issue_body:
I just installed aws-shell on Mac OS X Yosemite (10.10.5) and got the following error on first execution:

```
$ aws-shell
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/aws-shell", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('aws-shell==0.1.1', 'console_scripts', 'aws-shell')()
  File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Extras/lib/python/pkg_resources.py", line 357, in load_entry_point
    return get_distribution(dist).load_entry_point(group, name)
  File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Extras/lib/python/pkg_resources.py", line 2394, in load_entry_point
    return ep.load()
  File "/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/Extras/lib/python/pkg_resources.py", line 2108, in load
    entry = __import__(self.module_name, globals(),globals(), ['__name__'])
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/awsshell/__init__.py", line 7, in <module>
    from awsshell import shellcomplete
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/awsshell/shellcomplete.py", line 15, in <module>
    import botocore.session
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/botocore/session.py", line 26, in <module>
    import botocore.credentials
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/botocore/credentials.py", line 22, in <module>
    from dateutil.parser import parse
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/dateutil/parser.py", line 43, in <module>
    from . import tz
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/dateutil/tz/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    from .tz import *
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/dateutil/tz/tz.py", line 23, in <module>
    from ._common import tzname_in_python2, _tzinfo, _total_seconds
  File "/Library/Python/2.7/site-packages/dateutil/tz/_common.py", line 2, in <module>
    from six.moves import _thread
ImportError: cannot import name _thread
```

I solved by following the advice of [Matplotlib issue on OS X (“ImportError: cannot import name _thread”)](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/27630114/matplotlib-issue-on-os-x-importerror-cannot-import-name-thread) and changing the versions of `python-dateutil`.

```
sudo pip uninstall python-dateutil
sudo pip install python-dateutil==2.2
```

I'm not well versed in Python, but this looks related to the version of `six` and I wanted to post this issue to see if that was the right fix and it should be added to the README, or if it is something that should be fixed.

Deploying into Remote Machine
issue_body:
This is my use case: 

I have a web portal where my clients can upload instruction files to my gdrive folder. The only OAuth token needed is mine - my web server will upload everything via my oauth token. 

I am trying to understand how I can build my own custom oauth flow given that I am deploying my applications on remote machines (Heroku). 

Currently, I have settings.yaml as such: 
```
client_config_backend: settings
client_config:
  client_id: hello
  client_secret: world

save_credentials: True
save_credentials_backend: file
save_credentials_file: credentials.json

get_refresh_token: True

oauth_scope:
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file
  - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.install 
```

And I am running my oauth flow using the example:
```
self.gauth = GoogleAuth()
self.drive = GoogleDrive(self.gauth)
```

When I run this on my local machine, I am seeing a webpage firing up on my browser and asking me to authorize. Once I accept, my application works as expected. 

However, when I deploy this on Heroku, I am unable to visit the page fired. 

I tried using the following code: 
```
auth_url = gauth.GetAuthUrl() # Create authentication url user needs to visit
code = AskUserToVisitLinkAndGiveCode(auth_url) # Your customized authentication flow
gauth.Auth(code) # Authorize and build service from the code
```
The problem with this flow is that the gauth token can only be used once. And am I expected to authorize every time my webserver restarts? 

How can I replicate the flow on my webserver to the flow on my remote machines? Since `file` is the only option for `save_credentials_backend`, am I expected to manually upload the `credentials.json` to my webserver each time I deploy?

issue_title: making a link look like a button and button look like a link
issue_body: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/29235129-0fe4d52c-7eb1-11e7-89a9-f4eebe42d7a0.png) Currently on the sign-in page, we wrap `` in ``: ```html ``` resulting in a link containing a button: ```html ``` One of the effects is that as you tab through with the keyboard, both the link and the button get focus in turn. ---- Note that in the [original style guide doc](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HqbqIUxU0DriHXtKZag0AzeN8URDbDZzsSbc3zbdmag/edit?), we'd decided that from a user's perspective "links" and "buttons" can be used interchangeably. The difference is mainly an implementation detail. ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/2367265/29235174-7f7bc8f0-7eb1-11e7-8056-6fca3c0021f6.png) I believe the best long-term change might involve: * Change the `raised` prop on buttons to be `style`, and take a string that is one of `"raised"`, `"flat"`, or `"link"`. This will allow ``s to be styled as links. * Rename the style guide page "Buttons" to "Buttons and Links" * Create a new component `` which would be a wrapper around ``. It would take similar props to ``, in particular `text`, `primary`, ~`raised`~ `style`, and `disabled`. It would also include a new prop, `to`, which would be a proxy to the ``. It would _not_ include `type`, which is button-specific. Open question of whether it would emit a `click` event? After this, we would look for instances of `` and update them to use `` Of course, an alternative strategy is to stick with a single `` component which will render as a `` instead of `

None

0.10.0

issue_title: ipwbAdmin: Add ability control IPFS daemon
Logic for latest version does not account for versions deployed from 12-1am
issue_body:
The current version of ipwb is InterPlanetary Wayback 0.2017.07.04.0020.

`runningLatestIPWB()` in util.py currently performs a pretty naive version check by first fetching the version from pypi (which unfortunately assumes it's up and available!), then sanitizes the currently installed version via:
`currentVersion = ipwbVersion.replace('.0', '.')`

For the date-based format we used for versioning, this works for all fields except the last, which sanitizes to 0.2017.7.4.020. Because this is != to the exact version string (remember: naive!), the currently installed version is out of date.

To continue with the naïveté until the checking gets smarter, check for one or more zeros to be replaced in the sanitization process. A simple regex might be in-order.

issue_title: Skipping layers during build
issue_body:
When building the ibm-was-base layer [0]
I noticed that one of the layers to be merged were missing.
ibm-was-base uses layer ibm-im [1] which in turn uses ibm-base [2]. ibm-im is missing.

This is what it is happening. We first fetch the ibm-im layer in a temp directory under deps/layer/ https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/fetchers.py#L134 . Then we rename that temp directory to the name of the layer https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/fetchers.py#L58 (ibm-im). The InterfaceFetcher will extract https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/build/fetchers.py#L99 the last part of the repo path, wihich based on the interfaces entry [3] will be trunk and will rename the deps/layer/ibm-im to 
deps/layer/trunk https://github.com/juju/charm-tools/blob/master/charmtools/build/fetchers.py#L110
The next layer is ibm-base whose path also ends with trunk. Therefore it will overwrite the ibm-im layer already in the deps/layer/trunk .

Should the layers and interfaces be declared in a way in the  http://interfaces.juju.solutions/ so that we do not overwrite them while fetching or should we handle this in the charm-tools?

[0] http://bazaar.launchpad.net/~ibmcharmers/charms/trusty/layer-ibm-was-base/trunk/view/head:/layer.yaml
[1] https://launchpad.net/~ibmcharmers/charms/trusty/layer-ibm-im/trunk
[2] https://code.launchpad.net/~ibmcharmers/layer-ibm-base/trunk
[3] http://interfaces.juju.solutions/layer/ibm-im/

## What version am I running?

I ran the following command: `charm version` and got the following ouput:

```
charm 2.2.0-0ubuntu1~ubuntu16.04.1~ppa2
charm-tools 2.1.4
```

I am using: Ubuntu Xenial

issue_title: Definitions need to be stored with their Facades
issue_body:
Currently, in the client lib, we have a module called _definitions.py. This is a monolithic file containing "definition" classes for all of the Facades in  the_client<facade_version>.py modules. Where there is a conflict, the definition from the latest facade is used.

Right now, this happens to work, because all the definitions happen to be backwards compatible. This situation is fragile, however.

At the next opportunity, we need to refactor facade.py so that it stores definitions for each facade version separately, and then writes those definitions to a place only accessible to that facade version. The refactor is non trivial, and involves understanding and cleaning up the _types and _registry objects, as well as alerting the write_facades routine.

issue_body:
On a system with exabgp 3.4.13, healthcheck.py conflicts with python-enum34.

    [root@ext-dev-haproxy1 ~]# /usr/bin/python -m exabgp healthcheck --cmd "/etc/exabgp/healthchecks/https_healthcheck.sh api-dev.cloud.ipnett.se 443 /haproxy 185.19.66.177" --ip 185.19.66.177 --interval 30 --no-ip-setup --withdraw-on-down
    ERROR[healthcheck] Uncatched exception: __call__() takes at most 5 arguments (7 given)
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/exabgp/application/healthcheck.py", line 466, in main
        loop(options)
      File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/exabgp/application/healthcheck.py", line 338, in loop
        "DOWN",                 # Service is considered as down.
    TypeError: __call__() takes at most 5 arguments (7 given)

I suppose the fix is on import side.
Other details:

    [root@ext-dev-haproxy1 ~]# yum list installed | grep -E '^python(\.|.*addr|-enum)'
    python.x86_64                    2.7.5-34.el7                @rhel-7-server-rpms
    python-enum34.noarch             1.0.4-1.el7                 @rhel-7-server-rpms
    python-ipaddr.noarch             2.1.9-5.el7                 @rhel-7-server-rpms
    python-netaddr.noarch            0.7.5-7.el7                 @rhel-7-server-rpms

This is fixed in master. But there's no stable release with the fix yet. Any plans on that?

We generally prefer to try to reduce amount of projects that we build nightly in CI. And in the case of exabgp I'm not sure what the status is on 4.0/master anyway.
Anyway, the history is lost on https://github.com/Exa-Networks/exabgp/blob/master/lib/exabgp/application/healthcheck.py , but this part of the code does appear fixed in master

issue_title: DaemonApiMixin.events does not propagate HttpHeaders from config.json
issue_body: The docker.api.daemon.DaemonApiMixin.events does not make use of the config.json, which could have custom HTTP headers to pass to the server.

issue_title: How can I get the full exception in a failed build?
issue_body: I'm using this API and building images with `client.images.build(path="/../...", tag='foo:bar', quiet=False, rm=True)`. There is an exception being thrown in one of the DockerFile RUN statements, but the error returned just says `The command <....> returned a non-zero code: 1`. How do I get the full error from that exception?

issue_title: cpu_shares value can't be float.
issue_body:
if I use a float cpu_shares it will throw an exception as below:

> c.create_container(image='some/image', cpu_shares=0.8)                                                                  
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/client.py", line 239, in create_container
    return self.create_container_from_config(config, name)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/client.py", line 247, in create_container_from_config
    return self._result(res, True)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/clientbase.py", line 106, in _result
    self._raise_for_status(response)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/docker/clientbase.py", line 102, in _raise_for_status
    raise errors.APIError(e, response, explanation=explanation)
docker.errors.APIError: 500 Server Error: Internal Server Error ("json: cannot unmarshal number 0.8 into Go value of type int64")

docker version:

> Client version: 1.7.1
Client API version: 1.19
Package Version (client): docker-1.7.1-108.el7.centos.x86_64
Go version (client): go1.4.2
Git commit (client): 3043001/1.7.1
OS/Arch (client): linux/amd64
Server version: 1.7.1
Server API version: 1.19
Package Version (server): docker-1.7.1-108.el7.centos.x86_64
Go version (server): go1.4.2
Git commit (server): 3043001/1.7.1
OS/Arch (server): linux/amd64

docker-py:

> docker.version
'1.4.0-dev'

I think it's something about the changes in the Docker API.

issue_title: docker-py "Get http://: http: no Host in request URL" error when pulling docker image
issue_body:
From yesterday we cannot pull docker images using docker-py. It reports a missing http host error.

The closest related thing we've found is a missing `serveraddress` parameter value in docker remote API `/auth`: https://github.com/docker/docker/issues/22254

```
Client:
 Version:      1.12.3
 API version:  1.24
 Go version:   go1.6.3
 Git commit:   6b644ec
 Built:        Wed Oct 26 21:44:32 2016
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:      1.12.3
 API version:  1.24
 Go version:   go1.6.3
 Git commit:   6b644ec
 Built:        Wed Oct 26 21:44:32 2016
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

Docker now stores authentication data in `~/.docker/config.json`, but docker-py still relies on `~/.dockercfg`, so application based on docker-py can't access private repositories.

See docker/docker#12009 and docker/compose#1590

issue_title: Cannot pull from private registry with HTTP authentication
issue_body:
We have a private (v1) registry accessible via HTTPS which is protected with HTTP authentication (credentials set up via `.dockercfg`). Unfortunately, using docker-py to pull from this registry via
``` .py
import docker.client
client = docker.client.Client()
client.pull('our.registry.server:1234/bla', tag='latest')
```
doesn't work:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/client.py", line 747, in pull
    repository, insecure=insecure_registry
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/auth/auth.py", line 59, in resolve_repository_name
    return expand_registry_url(parts[0], insecure), parts[1]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/auth/auth.py", line 38, in expand_registry_url
    "HTTPS endpoint unresponsive and insecure mode isn't enabled."
docker.errors.DockerException: HTTPS endpoint unresponsive and insecure mode isn't enabled.
```
The problem is that in `auth.py`, `expand_registry_url` determins whether `https://our.registry.server:1234` works by accessing that URL (with something added) and aborting if any HTTP status code >= 400 is returned. Since we use HTTP authentication, this yields the HTTP status code 401, which causes the function to raise the above exception.

While this problem can be overcome in `expand_registry_url` by adding `https://`, its caller `resolve_repository_name` explicitly checks for that and bails out if a scheme is specified.

issue_title: Simplify `nifgen` and `nidcpower` trigger configuration
issue_body:
[Zen of Python](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0020/#id3):

> Simple is better than complex.

and 

> There should be one-- and preferably only one --obvious way to do it.

Based on this, we should get rid of methods:

```
us-aus-wireless-10-0-130-183:nimi-python kirsch$ grep -r def\ configure_digital_edge.*_trigger generated/
generated/nidcpower/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_measure_trigger(self, input_terminal, edge=enums.DigitalEdge.RISING):
generated/nidcpower/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_pulse_trigger(self, input_terminal, edge=enums.DigitalEdge.RISING):
generated/nidcpower/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_sequence_advance_trigger(self, input_terminal, edge=enums.DigitalEdge.RISING):
generated/nidcpower/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_source_trigger(self, input_terminal, edge=enums.DigitalEdge.RISING):
generated/nidcpower/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_start_trigger(self, input_terminal, edge=enums.DigitalEdge.RISING):
generated/nifgen/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_script_trigger(self, trigger_id, source, edge=enums.ScriptTriggerDigitalEdgeEdge.RISING):
generated/nifgen/session.py:    def configure_digital_edge_start_trigger(self, source, edge=enums.StartTriggerDigitalEdgeEdge.RISING):
```

But only after confirming that there are two corresponding properties for each trigger (please list below).

Also, the following attributes that aren't supported in 54x3 (older hardware is EOL):

* `digital_edge_script_trigger_edge`
* `digital_edge_start_trigger_edge `
* `configure_digital_level_script_trigger(self, trigger_id, source, trigger_when)`

Also, the following `nidcpower` attributes. NI-DCPower hardware routes all triggers via PXI backplane which defines pulses as rising edge only. Why allow configuring that?

* `digital_edge_measure_trigger_edge`
* `digital_edge_pulse_trigger_edge`
* `digital_edge_sequence_advance_trigger_edge`
* `digital_edge_source_trigger_edge`
* `digital_edge_start_trigger_edge`


Don't forget any accompanying add-on metadata (i.e. enums, defaults).

issue_title: ResourceWarning: unclosed transport [asyncio/websocket]
issue_body:
``` python
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/autobahn/asyncio/websocket.py", line 101, in connection_lost
    self.transport = None
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/asyncio/sslproto.py", line 327, in __del__
    warnings.warn("unclosed transport %r" % self, ResourceWarning)
  File "wampcams.py", line 25, in warn_with_traceback
    traceback.print_stack()
/usr/lib/python3.5/asyncio/sslproto.py:327: ResourceWarning: unclosed transport <asyncio.sslproto._SSLProtocolTransport object at 0x7f19f6a4a048>
  warnings.warn("unclosed transport %r" % self, ResourceWarning)
```

this is fixable with a quick single line, change WebSocketAdapterProtocol.connection_lost() at autobahn/asyncio/websocket.py:99 from this:

``` python
    def connection_lost(self, exc):
        self._connectionLost(exc)
        self.transport = None
```

to this:

``` python
    def connection_lost(self, exc):
        self._connectionLost(exc)
        self.transport.close()
        self.transport = None
```

issue_title: Music21Object.id problem
issue_body:
The id of all objects is settable, and this is great.  However, there are 538 places in the code where the id(object)  is used (object could be anything such as c, a, ...)  so this circumvents using the assigned ID.  

My initial discovery is due the fact that I assigned IDs when building up a score using loops and function calls, then in stream/core.py _addElementPreProcess id(element) is tested instead of element.id when writing to MusicXML or MIDI.

Python object ids are not guaranteed to be unique  during the life of the program so they should not be persistent across function calls.  

I suggest this initial fix:

in music21/base.py:
    `
     from uuid import uuid4
`
then at line 396 (or so):

```
        if "id" in keywords:
            self.id = keywords["id"]
        else:
            self.id = 'm21-'+str(uuid4())

```
This produces a unique id that can be persisted and used as needed. 

In stream/core.py line 280, replace

            idElement = id(element)

with

            idElement = element.id

also in the exception change id(element) to element.id 

I am sure that there are other issues like this lurking because of the id(object) checks.  But I haven't seen them yet. 


Thanks.

issue_title: MEI transposition information of instruments not parsed
issue_body:
It seems like the transposition information of instruments in an MEI file are not parsed.
Trying to get the actual sounding pitch of the notes always results in `music21.exceptions21.StreamException: atSoundingPitch is unknown: cannot transpose`

The MEI file does include the transpositions in a correct manner, for example `trans.semi="-7"`.

Minimal example:
```python
stream = converter.parseFile('Hummel_Concerto_for_trumpet.mei', None, "mei", True)
stream.toSoundingPitch(inPlace=True)
```

The MEI file used: [Hummel Concerto](http://www.verovio.org/examples/downloads/Hummel_Concerto_for_trumpet.mei)

issue_title: Behave Allure reports include Skipped tests when using --no-skipped or show_skipped = false
issue_body:
#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [ x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request

#### What is the current behavior?
Skipped issues appear in Allure Results even if Skipped `--no-skipped` flag is set or behave.ini is set to `show_skipped = false`.

#### Steps:
1. Create two senarios with different tags (for example 1 test tagged @chrome and another tagged @firefox). 
2. Run the tests with a feature --tag that will skip a test (`behave features/ --tags chrome --no-skipped`)

#### What is the expected behavior?
Skipped tests won't be included in allure results.

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?
In my case I'm trying to run chrome tests in CI against a selenium/standalone-chrome and Firefox against selenium/standalone-firefox docker images because my companies CI tool doesn't work with the Hub and Node configuration. And whichever runs last shows up as the most recent version. But really it just seems like if ignore skipped is turned on it should not be included in the result. 

#### Please tell us about your environment:

- Allure version:        2.6.0
- Test framework:     behave@1.2.6
- Allure adaptor:      allure-behave@2.3.2b1

issue_title: Use DSC replicate number directly as seed for RNG
issue_body: In the current implementation, the default seed for RNG is `DSC_REPLICATE_ID + SCRIPT_ID` where `SCRIPT_ID` is the current `md5sum` hash of the script file, converted to long-int. But I'm a bit bothered by the fact that each time I change the script, the results change, because the seed changes ... I think we should just use `DSC_REPLICATE_ID` as the default. If users need fancier behavior they should just set their own seeds.

issue_title: Conflicting expectations with homebrew on python
issue_body:
I'm running into some trouble calling `Python` with dsc installed with `pip3`.

Here's a little example of what's happening:

```yaml
# simple.dsc
foo: Python(import sys; print(sys.version); x = y)
  y: 1
  $x: x

DSC:
  define:
    first: foo
  run: first
  output: simple
```

Running:

```bash
$ dsc simple.dsc
INFO: DSC script exported to simple.html
INFO: Constructing DSC from simple.dsc ...
INFO: Building execution graph & running DSC ...
DSC:   0%|                                                                                                      | 0/2 [00:00<?, ?it/s]ERROR: Command 'python .sos/foo_0_0_f361fe52.py' returned non-zero exit status 1.
DSC:  50%|███████████████████████████████████████████████                                               | 1/2 [00:00<00:00,  1.84it/s]
ERROR: [c35a8e94-9b2e-44ca-b04a-000e255ebc60]: [foo_0]: 
Failed to execute python .sos/foo_0_0_f361fe52.py
exitcode=1, workdir={editted: unwieldy path name}/test_python, err=simple/foo/foo_1.stderr
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
[DSC]: 1 pending step: DSC_0
WARNING: Files in green in the error prompt above contains codes and error info to help debug.
Scripts upstream of the error can be found in simple.scripts.html.
INFO: Elapsed time 2.882 seconds.

$ cat simple/foo/foo_1.stderr
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/var/folders/bd/43q20k0n6z15tdfzxvd22r7c0000gn/T/tmpyrt9ig9o.py", line 4, in <module>
    from dsc.dsc_io import load_dsc as __load_dsc__
ImportError: No module named dsc.dsc_io
```

My guess is that `dsc` is expecting `python` to point to `python3`, while [the default for the homebrew package manager](https://docs.brew.sh/Homebrew-and-Python) is to have `python` point to `python2`.

I've been able to get this workflow to run using a conda environment where `python` does point to `python3`, but haven't been able to get it working by aliasing `python` to `python3`.

Thanks!

issue_title: Config in etcd not rolled back on healthcheck failure.
issue_body: If I add the `HEALTHCHECK_URL` setting to an application that subsequently fails its healthcheck, the release will be rolled back.  However, the etcd `/deis/config/app/healthcheck_url` key is not removed, which seems to cause the publisher to remove the existing containers from nginx, causing the app to 502 until someone fixes it.

issue_title: Healthcheck config changes aren't removed from etcd
issue_body:
I've strange issue with my deisv1, we've had healthchecks turned on once in the past, but it was failing with more than 3-4 containers, so we've turned it off, and removed all healthcheck related config values. However from time to time, application is failing to start because healthcheck error, there is no healthcheck-related configuration when running `deis config -a <app name>`, but after looking directly into etcd I've noticed this:

```
$ etcdctl ls deis/config/app | grep health
/deis/config/app/healthcheck_url
/deis/config/app/healthcheck_initial_delay
/deis/config/app/healthcheck_timeout
```

I remember we've removed it manualy from etcd in the past (etcdctl rm...), but now it's back. I've thought it might be related to recent controller restart (IIRC all etcd config values are propagated on restart) I've also looked into Deis postgresql database, and config attached to this particular app doesn't have any healthcheck related settings.

Any advice on this would be greatly appreciated, because it breaks our production environement from time to time.

issue_title: Can not use official nginx docker image on port 80
issue_body:
Simple `Dockerfile`:
```
FROM nginx
EXPOSE 80
```
Even if `EXPOSE` has been rewritted, docker expose 2 ports:
`0.0.0.0:32779->80/tcp, 0.0.0.0:32780->443/tcp`
Publisher publish the port 443 on etcd:
```sh
$ etcdctl get /deis/services/test-docker-nginx/test-docker-nginx_v4.cmd.1
10.10.8.10:32779
```
But container does not listen on 443 and the routeur send 502 Bad Gateway.

For testing, just replace etcd value:
```sh
$ etcdctl set /deis/services/test-docker-nginx/test-docker-nginx_v2.cmd.1 "10.10.8.10:32778"
10.10.8.10:32778
```

issue_title: Move globus transfer acl commands to be an endpoint subcommand
issue_body:
ACLs are secondary resources on endpoints (S3 and Shared), so they should be namespaced inside of `globus transfer endpoint acl`.

Karl suggested this, and I pushed back slightly, thinking they should be in `globus transfer share acl`.
When he informed me that S3 eps can have ACLs, that convinced me.
No one else seems to have a strong opinion.

issue_title: Add support for setting endpoint subscription_id on create/update
issue_body:
The Transfer API supports setting `subscription_id` on create, but not on update.
We discussed resolving this, and it seems that support in update will be added in the next release.

Opening this to supersede existing issues. Blocked until Transfer API releases the update support.
Not considered a 1.0 requirement.

issue_title: Enforce UUID Type on all Endpoint IDs
issue_body:
Currently, canonical names can make their way through the CLI and to the backend.

There's no need to support this into the future, so for the sake of future iterations of the CLI, we should strictly enforce that all endpoint IDs are UUIDs.

issue_title: Add protection on `globus delete` against unsafe globs?
issue_body:
This was discussed on #69 
Deletes through the new CLI are always `--unsafe` per hosted CLI rules.

Should we add client-side protections against trailing globs?

issue_title: Consistently de-capitalize "Endpoint", "Bookmark", and "Share"
issue_body: Per discussion, these proper nouns should be lowercased.

issue_title: Add Wagon version to metadata
issue_body:
To allow users to know which version of Wagon creates a specific wagon, we should provide it with each wagon's metadata. This will allow users to handle backward compatibility issues.

We can use:

```python
import pkg_resources
wagon_version = pkg_resources.get_distribution('wagon').version
```

to get the version and then append it in the metadata: `created_with: 'wagon-{0}'.format(version)`

issue_title: Liskov Violation in `HStoreDict`
issue_body:
First off, I am using this library with `schema_mode` turned off.

Now, it is very likely that my code will have the built-in assumption that, 

For all `x`
If `isinstance(d, dict)`, 
and I type `d["hi"] = x`
then `d["hi"] == x`

This is guaranteed in general by dictionaries, but not by `HStoreDict` (since everything gets converted to strings).  This can cause very [subtle errors](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liskov_substitution_principle#A_typical_violation).

In my case, the [Voluptuous library](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/voluptuous/) was failing to properly coerce strings to floats, because it was consuming an `HStoreDict`.  The workaround is just to convert my `HStoreDict` back to a standard `dict` before passing it on to Voluptuous.

I am not sure if there is a sensible way to fix this problem.  I imagine that the this behavior exists because the `hstore` column stores only strings; if they are going to have to be saved in the DB as strings, we might as well get that work done as early as possible.

Perhaps we could move to a strategy where we convert values to strings on save, rather than on dictionary-update?  Or maybe we could raise an exception if you try to insert a non-string into the dictionary?

issue_title: Serialize to EDS
issue_body:
Elementary Dependency Structures get a bit of usage, so pyDelphin should put more effort into getting these right. We already have a rudimentary (and incorrect) serializer, but it needs better support for QEQs and "disconnected" graphs. See the LKB's Lisp code for its head-finding routine, which is more sophisticated than the DMRS one (though I'm not suggesting the DMRS code should use it).

Since EDS are lossy, this issue is only about serializing TO EDS, not deserializing from them.

issue_title: Allow and/or document preprocessor usage with the ACE interface
issue_body:
gTest allows preprocessors in the ART command like this:

```python
ace_cmd = '{prep}ace -g {gram} {opts}'.format(
            prep=ace_preprocessor + ' | ' if ace_preprocessor else '',
            gram=grm,
            opts=' '.join(ace_options or [])
        )
        subprocess.check_call(
            ['art', '-a', ace_cmd, dest_dir] + (options or []),
            stdout=log, stderr=log, close_fds=True
        )
```

But it's not possible, or at least not clear how, to use a preprocessor in pyDelphin with the ACE interface directly.

issue_title: wish: for auto proxy sites, process proxy.pac
Unsatisfiable dependencies only when python version specifier spans across major versions
issue_body:
We recently wrote a (currently Windows only) recipe for GDCM, and uploaded packages for python version 2.6 through 3.5, 64 bit and 32 bit. Here's [the recipe](https://github.com/ClinicalGraphics/conda-recipes/blob/master/gdcm/meta.yaml) and here's [the package on Anaconda.org](http://anaconda.org/clinicalgraphics/gdcm/files).

Its features and requirements are short and simple, here's the relevant part of the recipe:

```yaml
build:
  features:
    - vc9  # [win and (py26 or py27)]
    - vc10  # [win and (py33 or py34)]
    - vc14  # [win and py35]

requirements:
  run:
    - python
```

Now, when creating an environment with only `python` and `gdcm`, in many configurations, you get an "unsatisfiable dependencies" error, while clearly there is a `gdcm` package available for any version of `python` on Windows.

```
> conda create -c clinicalgraphics -n myenv python gdcm
Fetching package metadata: ........
Solving package specifications: .
Error: Unsatisfiable package specifications.
Generating hint:
[      COMPLETE      ]|##################################################| 100%


Hint: 'gdcm' has unsatisfiable dependencies (see 'conda info gdcm')
```

```
> conda create -c clinicalgraphics -n example "python>=3.3*" gdcm              
Fetching package metadata: ........                                            
Solving package specifications: ....                                           
Error: Unsatisfiable package specifications.                                   
Generating hint:                                                               
[      COMPLETE      ]|##################################################| 100%
                                                                               
                                                                               
Hint: 'gdcm' has unsatisfiable dependencies (see 'conda info gdcm')            
                                                                               
Note that the following features are enabled:                                  
  - vc10                                                                       
  - vc14                                                                       
```

Surprisingly, this works just fine:

```
> conda create -c clinicalgraphics -n example python=2* gdcm
Fetching package metadata: ........
Solving package specifications: ...........
Package plan for installation in environment C:\Users\korij\Miniconda3\envs\example:

The following packages will be downloaded:

    package                    |            build
    ---------------------------|-----------------
    setuptools-19.2            |           py27_0         651 KB

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    gdcm:         2.6.1-py27_vc9_7 [vc9]
    msvc_runtime: 1.0.1-vc9_0      [vc9]
    pip:          7.1.2-py27_0
    python:       2.7.11-0
    setuptools:   19.2-py27_0
    wheel:        0.26.0-py27_1

Proceed ([y]/n)?
```

```
> conda create -c clinicalgraphics -n example python=3.5* gdcm                      
Fetching package metadata: ........                                                 
Solving package specifications: .............                                       
Package plan for installation in environment C:\Users\korij\Miniconda3\envs\example:
                                                                                    
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:                                       
                                                                                    
    gdcm:         2.6.1-py35_vc14_7 [vc14]                                          
    msvc_runtime: 1.0.1-vc14_0      [vc14]                                          
    pip:          7.1.2-py35_0

Proceed ([y]/n)?

Any thoughts?

issue_title: Re-creating conda env with custom channels results in HTTP error (perhaps needlessly?)
issue_body:
**I'm submitting a...** - [X] bug report - [X] feature request ## Current Behavior I created an environment on one machine and wanted to replicate that environment. On the original machine, I exported the environment details using: `conda env export > my-env.yml`. On the new machine, running `conda env create -n my-env-copy --file my-env.yml` resulted in at HTTP error (as was the case in #7535): ```shell CondaHTTPError: HTTP 000 CONNECTION FAILED for url ``` ### Steps to Reproduce Create a new environment from an exported yaml file, which includes an http address under the channels sub-section. ## Expected Behavior The channel should not only be searched for via the given http address in the `env.yml` file, but also perhaps via pure channel name, which I guess can often be parsed from the http address. In my case, the http was: `- https://conda.binstar.org/bashtage` I changed the same line in the yaml file to `bashtage` - which then worked as expected. Could a fix/patch be as simple as something like: ```python channel = ''https://conda.binstar.org/bashtage" # from yaml-reader try: # whatever normally happens: except CondaHTTPError as e: channel_name = channel.split('/')[-1] # overwrite the variable that is passed to the usual method in the try-block above # ... try again, which would either work or raise the same CondaHTTPError # and we don't catch it the second time around! finally: # in case of success using name, and we want to keep users informed... warnings.warn('A message alerting user that channel name was used instead of http address') ``` Alternatively, all this could be performed via the export process, while _creating_ the yaml file. I assume the http address was chosen because it should be static/more robust? ## Environment Information
`conda info`

This was creating a new environment, so it not specific to any environment. ``` nicholas@mitchell:~$ conda info active environment : None user config file : /home/nicholas/.condarc populated config files : conda version : 4.5.5 conda-build version : 3.0.27 python version : 3.6.3.final.0 base environment : /home/nicholas/anaconda3 (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/linux-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/noarch package cache : /home/nicholas/anaconda3/pkgs /home/nicholas/.conda/pkgs envs directories : /home/nicholas/anaconda3/envs /home/nicholas/.conda/envs platform : linux-64 user-agent : conda/4.5.5 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.3 Linux/4.15.0-24-generic ubuntu/16.04 glibc/2.23 UID:GID : 1000:1000 netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

issue_title: fromdataframe column number issues
issue_body:
Hi all. I'm trying out this module in Python3 (using Anaconda installer on Windows), and I'm having some troubles with DataFrame input. It seems if the number of columns is greater than 254, then you get an error. Example below. Change the ncols to < 255 and this works fine. Any thoughts on what I can do about this?

```
import bcolz
import string
import numpy as np

ncols = 300
nrows = 100
def namer():
    return ''.join(np.random.choice(list(string.ascii_letters), size=6))
col1 = np.array([[namer(),] for i in range(nrows)])
col2 = np.array([[namer(),] for i in range(nrows)])

names = [namer() for i in range(ncols-2)]
datacols = np.random.rand(100,ncols-2)
df = pd.DataFrame(datacols, columns=names)

df['AAA_0'] = col1
df['AAA_1'] = col2
df = df.reindex_axis(sorted(df.columns), axis=1)

bcolz.ctable.fromdataframe(df)
```
Produces the following error:
```
  File "<string>", line 12
SyntaxError: more than 255 arguments
```
I can "trick" the ctable formation (using `df` from above):
```
data = bcolz.ctable.fromdataframe(df.iloc[:,[0]])
for col in df.iloc[:,1:]:
    data.addcol(df[col].values, name=df[col].name, dtype=df[col].dtype)
```
But now the dtypes of the columns are not equivalent:
```
In : data[0]
Out: (b'MFBTAz', 'MDrfoV', 0.22661330919358336, ....
```

I am doing the following:

    vcf_file = pysam.VariantFile(vcf_file_name)
    for rec in vcf_file.fetch():

issue_title: cannot drop view whily syncing
issue_body:
Hi,
we saw that there is an issue with syncing views, in case you change views that are dependencies for other views:

#### How to reproduce the error
1) create 2 Views like this
```
class Test1View(django_pgviews.View):
    sql = "SELECT last_name, email from auth_user;"

    class Meta:
        db_table = 'myapp_test1view'
        managed = False


class Test2View(django_pgviews.View):
    dependencies = [
        'myapp.Test1View',
    ]
    sql = "SELECT email from common_test1view;"

    class Meta:
        db_table = 'common_test2view'
        managed = False
```
2) run `manage.py sync_pgviews --force`

3) change the sql of `Test1View` to be `SELECT last_name, first_name, email from auth_user;`

4) run `manage.py sync_pgviews --force`
--> You will encounter the following error during sync:
`psycopg2.InternalError: cannot drop view myapp_test1view because other objects depend on it`



I will try to come up with a fix for this, unless you know another way how to get around this error.

issue_title: Allow Repository Validation to be disabled
issue_body:
Currently, when a snapshot is used, the repository verification API is invoked. If there's a hiccup in this check, then it prevents the snapshot. It's a pretty reasonable check to make and it should certainly be default, but sometimes the failure is worth trying to go around (particularly with debugging).

```
curator snapshot --skip-repo-validation
```

issue_title: curator plugin is not working when I enable http basic auth on elastic search 1.4 curator
A bit too hidden "ignore" parameter.
issue_body:
I'm trying pylint today, and reviewing my code with it, it shows a `Unexpected keyword argument 'ignore' in method call [E1123]`, when I call the `indices.create` method.

And it seems legit, the create method is defined as:
```
    @query_params('timeout', 'master_timeout')
    def create(self, index, body=None, params=None):
        """
        Create an index in Elasticsearch.
        `<http://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-create-index.html>`_

        :arg index: The name of the index
        :arg body: The configuration for the index (`settings` and `mappings`)
        :arg master_timeout: Specify timeout for connection to master
        :arg timeout: Explicit operation timeout
        """
```

There's no way here, even for the developper, to know that it's possible to give an `ignore` parameter here. [I know it's documented](https://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.org/en/master/api.html#ignore), does documenting it here is enough to completly hide its existance in the methode where it's possible to use it ?

issue_title: Examples won't always work when copy and pasted
issue_body:
After a talk with @twitchax about examples and feedback we've received regarding the ability to copy and paste examples and have them reliably work, I think we might be able to achieve this by making examples a little more dynamic. I think we have a few issues to overcome.
- execution environment (bash, ps, cmd)
- globally defined resources (dns entries -- storage accounts, web apps, etc -- others)
- rules for parameter values (password strength, account name requirements, etc)

If the examples were dynamic enough to produce content for the above criteria, I think we could ensure that example commands would execute reliably (within reason) when copied and pasted.

Thoughts? @azure/azure-cli

issue_title: Tip: Use your Azure CLI login session to connect to Active Directory and use the AzureAD cmdletes
issue_body:
I got tired of having to log in to Azure in so many different ways since all tooling have some unique functionality (Azure CLI, AzureAD for PS, AzureRM for PS) and when I found the get-access-token command here I got intrigued and actually managed to log in to the AD Graph.

Here's some PS code:

$subscriptions = (az account list -o json 2> $null | ConvertFrom-Json)
# Get a token for AD (Graph)
$adtoken_container = (az account get-access-token --resource "https://graph.windows.net/ " | ConvertFrom-Json)
$adtoken = $adtoken_container.accessToken

$first_sub = $subscriptions[0]
$user_name = $first_sub.user.name
$tenant = $first_sub.tenantId

Connect-AzureAD -aadaccesstoken "$adtoken" -accountid "$user_name" -tenantid "$tenant" 

Get-AzureADUser -top 1 # proves that it worked

issue_title: UserPassCredentials/ServicePrincipal should check "resource" ends with "/"
issue_body:
For ARM endpoint, "resource" has to be terminated by a / to avoid that: https://github.com/AzureAD/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python/issues/60

Default with public Azure here uses it: 'https://management.core.windows.net/'
It's not necessary for not-ARM stuff, like https://graph.windows.net, but it's necessary for ARM not public azure cloud (like Gov).

Checking the / at the end does not hurt GraphAPI or KeyVault and makes multi-cloud authentication simplier,

I don't have a preference if we raise exception, or add "/" automatically if not present.

issue_title: TimeoutError: Proper way to read chunk wise?
issue_body:
## Long story short

- When fetching files chunk-wise, we get `TimeoutError` - dependent on hardware (processing power, chunk size) sooner or later. E.g. On a MBP 2017, Python 3.65 + aiohttp 3.3 while reading a 10GB file chunk wise at 512KB per `read`. On an embedded Linux w/ limited processing power + 512 bytes chunk size much earlier (e.g. 512Bytes / 80MB file).
- See https://github.com/rauc/rauc-hawkbit/pull/8. Which chunk size makes sense? Can we just catch it and retry reading or is this a bad idea?
- Same happens with using `read_chunk` - what's the proper way to read chunk wise?

## Expected behaviour

- No `TimeoutError` exception

## Actual behaviour

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "aiohttp-rauc-hawkbit.py", line 30, in <module>
    loop.run_until_complete(main())
  File "/usr/local/opt/asdf/installs/python/3.6.5/lib/python3.6/asyncio/base_events.py", line 468, in run_until_complete

issue_title: FileResponse failed to prepare proper headers for Range requests
issue_body:
## Long story short
Please describe your problem and why the fix is important.

Users may pause their downloads of large static resources.
Many download managers including those browser-builtin ones check for Content-Range headers. Missing this header would cause downloads to fail.
I checked the source code and found out static resources are handled by FileResponse in web_fileresponse.py. But {0}.format(__title__)

## Expected behaviour
What is the behaviour you expect?

Assume you have a BIG file big_file
big_file.size() == 10000 (9.77kb)
A user downloaded 500 bytes (0-499) and connection lost.
And then the user resumes download with request: "Range:bytes=500-"
Expected header: "Content-Range:bytes 500-9999/10000"   <- this one is missing

## Actual behaviour
What's actually happening?

The downloads just failed immediately after clicking resume button.
Actually aiohttp handles Range requests very well. HTTP 206 and Content-Length are both correctly returned to the user.

## Steps to reproduce
Please describe steps to reproduce the issue.
     If you have a script that does that please include it here within
     markdown code markup

Check the web_fileresponse.py source code, or try a download like this
from aiohttp import web
import asyncio
loop = asyncio.get_event_loop
app = web.Application(loop = loop)
app.add_static('/', folder_containing_large_file, show_index = True)
web.run_app(app, host = '0.0.0.0', port = 4321)

## Your environment

Describe the environment you have that lead to your issue.
     This includes aiohttp version, OS, proxy server and other bits that
     are related to your case. 
     
     IMPORTANT: aiohttp is both server framework and client library.
     For getting rid of confusing please put 'server', 'client' or 'both'
     word here.

Server
Python/3.6.4
aiohttp/3.0.9

## Temporary workaround
in file web_fileresponse.py:
  line 228 == "if count:"
in line 227 I added:
  self.headers[hdrs.ACCEPT_RANGES] = 'bytes'
(Accept-Ranges header, which is to indicate that the server supports Range requests, is also missing. But I  found out that usually web clients just ignore it. Even though this one is missing, they still send Range requests. Maybe an Accept-Range:none header is needed?)
in line 229 I added:
  if start is not None: self.headers[hdrs.CONTENT_RANGE] = f'bytes {start}-{start+count-1}/{file_size}'
(After this line is added, download managers work like a charm)
These two lines could be a temporary workaround. It has problems left listed as below

## Other things to check
Some download managers uses this header:
  If-Range:xxxxxx
  Range:bytes=yyyy-zzzz
And some uses this (including Chrome browser):
  If-Unmodified-Since:xxxxxx
  Range:bytes=yyyy-zzzz
Both are not properly handled by FileResponse. Nothing is checked and HTTP 206 is returned with requested partial content
As far as I know, what is expected:
if condition:
  return HTTP 206, Content-Length = len(requested_part), Content-Range=start-end/full_length
else:
  return HTTP 200, Content-Length = full_length

(EOF. It's my first time using github, please tell me if anything wrong. English is not my first language, please point out if any mistake. Sorry about that)

issue_title: Add max_message_size for websockets (both client and server)
issue_body:
## Feature

Aiohttp has no limit on websocket message sizes. I had a brief discussion in the google-group about this and decided to create this issue.

That looks pretty dangerous, as it allows to DoS/OOM the server with a single client without any effort at all (you can implement this 'killer' client on a simple socket API without expending the memory yourself).

Closest 'conceptual' relative of aiohttp (IMHO) aka tornado has this limit both on client and on server APIs:

http://www.tornadoweb.org/en/stable/websocket.html#tornado.websocket.WebSocketHandler
http://www.tornadoweb.org/en/stable/websocket.html#tornado.websocket.WebSocketClientConnection

It cannot be implemented on the application layer - it's too late, message has been fully loaded already. This limit must be deep in protocol implementation, where websocket fragmented messages are collected.

ps. I understand that it can probably alleviated by using a production reverse proxy, but for pure WS workload direct connection seems reasonable too.

pps I'm not sure if I'll be able to produce a PR on this anytime soon, but it seems to be important for production use of aio websockets (as is #1845, shameless plug xD).

## Possible implementation

Proposal: Add max_message_size parameter both to ClientSession.ws_connect and to WebSocketResponse, which will reject oversized messages right on the header parsing stage (and either close the connection or emit the WSMsgType.Error message). As a bonus, the same parameter can be added to the Application, that would serve as a default value for the WebSocketResponse one (if it's possible).

Another possible solution would be to expose some kind of streaming API. IMHO that would be more complicated both for the user and for the implementer, but it's would also work.

issue_title: When add_shortcut is called in a loop, all shortcuts will invoke the same callback.
issue_body:
The following code should replace the current selection with “1” when pressing <kbd>F1</kbd>,  “2” when pressing <kbd>F2</kbd>, …:

```js
const nums = Array.from(new Array(12), (_, i) => i + 1)
const replace_with = i => Jupyter.notebook.get_selected_cell().code_mirror.replaceSelection(i)

nums.forEach(i =>
  Jupyter.keyboard_manager.edit_shortcuts.add_shortcut(
    `F${i}`, () => replace_with(i)))
```

However, `keyboard_manager` works through a indirection via action IDs. `add_shortcut` creates an ID for callbacks via `callback.toString()`. Since this creates the same ID for all invocations of `add_shortcut` here, the callback for that ID is set and then replaced 11 times. So pressing F1 through F12 always inserts “12”.

I got a complex notebook, with many extensions inside an Anaconda environment. For example and importantly the initialization cells. The notebook also contains initialization cells. I also got the extension for hidden cells, since I want the user to focus on the actual matter, rather than the details of how I rendered a PDF in a notebook cell for example. Do I need to use a different way of installing libraries and the notebook in my Anaconda env, to work around this problem? Maybe specifying a development/beta version of some jupyter related library?

Note: When I do not run the `jupyter trust` command, the notebook will load fine, only that it says that there are initialization cells in an untrusted notebook, sort of forcing the users to run them themselves, which would lead to unnecessary distraction.

issue_title: improved completions with type hinting
issue_body:
The `autocomplete-plus` package for the Atom editor (recently bundled into Atom) shows how powerful [types in autocomplete](https://github.com/atom/autocomplete-plus/pull/334) can be.

![atom completions](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/69169/6793515/b505ee3a-d182-11e4-9b9f-74b2a5d77365.png)

Something similar would be a huge boon to Jupyter. Instead of just showing text matches for variables in the running kernel (already very powerful!), show what they are: a variable of type String, a function that takes two integers and returns a float, etc. 

This would be a huge step forward for interactive programming of interpreted languages, and would truly showcase the power of having a live kernel to introspect into.

issue_title: I found a bug, I don't know if it is documented
issue_body:
I found a bug, I don't know if it is documented

If you have a file named `code.py` in the directory that you run `$ jupyter notebook` in, you will get many errors and kernel death, even when opening a new (empty) notebook.

Delete or move the file named `code.py` and the issues magically go away.

issue_title: Wishlist: planemo test with XML tool file coverage
issue_body:
I've been starting to take advantage of Python code coverage tools lately, which work by hooking into the Python interpreter (much like profiling tools) and record which lines in your code are actually exercised when running the test suite. This is usually summarised as a percentage score (fraction of lines of code tested, comments not counted).

It would be nice to have something similar for Galaxy tools assessing how much of the tool XML file is actually exercised in the tests. The Cheetah syntax in the ``<command>``  tag is probably the hardest and would need a similarly invasive approach. Likewise the ``<configfile>`` tag.

For now I think focusing on static analysis via ``planemo lint`` seems most practical.

issue_title: Non-homogeneousness of the module implemented by Sonnet.
issue_body:
This is probably not a defect of sonnet, however, I would like to share it to see on earth it is a potential bug or not.

Currently I implemented a multi-layer RNN model. The cell is intended of some customized one, the code is as follows:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import sonnet as snt

class Cell(snt.RNNCore):
    def __init__(self, hidden_size, name = "cell"):
        super(Cell, self).__init__(name = name)
        self._hidden_size = hidden_size
        with self._enter_variable_scope():
            self._update_lin = snt.Linear(output_size = hidden_size)
            self._reset_lin = snt.Linear(output_size = hidden_size)
            self._cell_seq = snt.Sequential([snt.Linear(output_size = hidden_size), tf.tanh])

    def _build(self, inputs, state):
        i_s = tf.concat([inputs, state], -1)
        reset = self._reset_lin(i_s)
        update = self._update_lin(i_s)
        cell = self._cell_seq(tf.concat([inputs, reset * state], -1))
        output = update * state + (1 - update) * cell

        return output, output

    @property
    def state_size(self):
        return tf.TensorShape([self._hidden_size])

    @property
    def output_size(self):
        return tf.TensorShape([self._hidden_size])

    def initial_state(self, batch_size):
        return tf.zeros([batch_size, self._hidden_size])

```

However, when I try to use the code to build a multi-layer RNN, the following way is work:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import sonnet as snt
from cell import Cell

class Model(snt.AbstractModule):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, num_layers = 2, hidden_size = 8, name = "model"):
        super(Model, self).__init__(name = name)
        self._batch_size = batch_size
        self._num_layers = num_layers
        self._hidden_size = hidden_size
        with self._enter_variable_scope():
            self._cell1 = Cell(8)
            self._cell2 = Cell(8)
            # self._cells = tf.nn.rnn_cell.MultiRNNCell([Cell(8)] * num_layers, state_is_tuple = True)
            self._seq = snt.Sequential([snt.Linear(output_size = 1), tf.tanh])

    def _build(self, inputs):

        init_state = self._cell1.initial_state(self._batch_size)
        outputs, _ = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cell1, inputs, initial_state = init_state)

        init_state = self._cell2.initial_state(self._batch_size)
        outputs, _ = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cell2, outputs, initial_state = init_state)

        '''
        init_state = self._cells.zero_state(self._batch_size, tf.float32)
        outputs, states = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cells, inputs, initial_state = init_state)
        '''

        outputs = tf.unstack(outputs, axis = 1)
        outputs = self._seq(outputs[-1])

        return outputs

```

But the way below is not, which is believed equivalent to the above approach:

```
import tensorflow as tf
import sonnet as snt
from cell import Cell

class Model(snt.AbstractModule):
    def __init__(self, batch_size, num_layers = 2, hidden_size = 8, name = "model"):
        super(Model, self).__init__(name = name)
        self._batch_size = batch_size
        self._num_layers = num_layers
        self._hidden_size = hidden_size
        with self._enter_variable_scope():
            # self._cell1 = Cell(8)
            # self._cell2 = Cell(8)
            self._cells = tf.nn.rnn_cell.MultiRNNCell([Cell(8)] * num_layers, state_is_tuple = True)
            self._seq = snt.Sequential([snt.Linear(output_size = 1), tf.tanh])

    def _build(self, inputs):

        '''
        init_state = self._cell1.initial_state(self._batch_size)
        outputs, _ = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cell1, inputs, initial_state = init_state)

        init_state = self._cell2.initial_state(self._batch_size)
        outputs, _ = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cell2, outputs, initial_state = init_state)

        '''
        init_state = self._cells.zero_state(self._batch_size, tf.float32)
        outputs, states = tf.nn.dynamic_rnn(self._cells, inputs, initial_state = init_state)


        outputs = tf.unstack(outputs, axis = 1)
        outputs = self._seq(outputs[-1])

        return outputs

```

You can test above code using the following code snippet:

```
def test():

    import os
    os.environ["CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES"]="0"

    t = tf.constant([[[1], [2], [3], [4], [5], [6]],
                 [[2], [3], [4], [5], [6], [7]],
                 [[3], [4], [5], [6], [7], [8]],
                 [[4], [5], [6], [7], [8], [9]]], tf.float32)

    model = Model(4)
    r = model(t)

    with tf.Session() as sess:
        sess.run(tf.global_variables_initializer())
        v = sess.run(r)
        print(v)

if __name__ == "__main__":

```

issue_title: RFC: new API
issue_body:
I've been rethinking the pypuppetdb API and how much work it is to support new endpoints and new options. I came up with a new design that I like far more and is far more flexible. It currently works like this:

``` python
>>> from pypuppetdb.client import Client
>>> client = Client()
>>> client.host
'localhost'
>>> client.port
8080
>>> client.ssl
{'key': None, 'cert': None, 'verify': True}
>>> client.timeout
10
>>> client.protocol
'http'
```

So far this is mostly the same except for that `client.ssl` is now a dict with its settings combined.

Now, to query an endpoint:

``` python
>>> client.endpoint('nodes').path('hostname').path('facts')
Client(host=localhost, port=8080, ssl_verify=True, ssl_cert=None, ssl_key=None, timeout=10)
```

The `.endpoint()` and `.path()` methods on `Client` return `self` meaning we can now chain methods. So instead of needing to provide special methods per endpoint you can just chain an `endpoint()` and multiple `path()` calls on the `client`.

This means that whenever an endpoint is added to PuppetDB's API we have to do… absolutely nothing!

If you want to clear the endpoint and path entries, call `clear()`:

``` python
>>> client.endpoint('nodes')
Client(host=localhost, port=8080, ssl_verify=True, ssl_cert=None, ssl_key=None, timeout=10)
>>> client.path('facts')
Client(host=localhost, port=8080, ssl_verify=True, ssl_cert=None, ssl_key=None, timeout=10)
>>> client.path()
'facts'
>>> client.endpoint()
'nodes'
>>> client.clear()
Client(host=localhost, port=8080, ssl_verify=True, ssl_cert=None, ssl_key=None, timeout=10)
>>> client.endpoint()
>>> client.path()
>>>
```

To actually query the endpoint you simply chain `.query()` to it:

``` python
>>> client.query()
'http://localhost:8080/v4/nodes/hostname/facts'
```

Right now it just returns the URL it would have queried but obviously that's not the final implementation.

The idea is that `query()` would return a QuerySet-like object supporting the usual Python slicing operators, `[1:5]`, be an iterator etc. The QuerySet would be the one to contain the knowledge on result pagination and would by default fetch about 20 results (I think, maybe more).

I'm going to add a method that allows you to set the return object you expect. If it's not set the QuerySet will simply throw the JSON back at you, if it is set it will decode the JSON into the object so we can maintain the convenience object like Node with additional methods.

issue_title: Product Structure and Attributes
issue_body:
Hi all

I've now been hard at work implementing our new shop in django oscar. Sadly django-oscar isn't very good at product variations and attributes. I'm considering how to fix this, and would like some input from the community.

Imo the best and simplest implementation would be to have every product be a variation, even if it's a standalone product. This greatly simplifies working with products all around the framework, and would make product editing in the dashboard more intuitive. You could even change the layout in the dashboard if only a single variation is created.

I've made a little list of things that the variation system should be able to handle.

  - Variant generation from attributes set in product class
  - No duplicate variants should be allowed
  - Multi inline editing of variants
  - Image on variants (for automatic image switching on product page)

Regarding Attributes i have some ideas as well. First order of business would be to unify Options and Attributes. It's confusing that these are 2 separate entities. This would entail removing the AbstractOption model, this is already confusing since the only way to add options is through django admin. There should be a global entry to manage attributes. Looking through the current models, it seems like much of this can be implemented right away, with a few modifications.  

and here is a list of the thing attributes should able to handle

  - Attributes should have it's own global management, and then be attached to product classes
  - Swatch type on the ProductAttributeValue (for handling frontend representation e.g. Color, Image etc.)

There's probably more stuff that i forgot in this writeup, so please let me know if missed anything and if you are interested in helping out implementing. Obviously much of the work will be in the frontend, and i think i read somewhere that the dashboard was about to be rewritten, so if anyone has information on this i would be glad to have it. 

Cheers

issue_title: System.Threading.Overlapped fails to install in a Xamarin project
issue_body:
# NuGet package `System.Threading.Overlapped` fails to install in a Xamarin project

## General

Xamarin projects are still using `packages.config` and the _old_ NuGet system, so no `PackageReference` in the `.csproj`. I think that is why it is still looking for a `lib` folder with .NET Standard assemblies and why it is interpreting the NuGet package incorrectly.

I experienced this with an Android app, however looks like it doesn't really matter which kind of Xamarin project you're using as described in https://github.com/xamarin/xamarin-macios/issues/3138

I manually added the package using a `PackageReference` in my csproj, which succeeded. But then the app crashes at runtime when trying to load the assemblies.

The other workaround to avoid the Win32 files mentioned in [the issue](https://github.com/xamarin/xamarin-macios/issues/3138) didn't work for me and failed with the following compilation error:

```
/Library/Frameworks/Mono.framework/Versions/5.4.1/lib/mono/xbuild/Microsoft/NuGet/Microsoft.NuGet.targets(5,5): Error: Your project.json doesn't have a runtimes section. You should add '"runtimes": { "unix": { } }' to your project.json and then re-run NuGet restore.
```

## Repro

Just create any Xamarin project (Android app, iOS app...) and try to install `System.Threading.Overlapped` with Visual Studio for Mac.

## Version info

```
=== Visual Studio Enterprise 2017 for Mac ===

Version 7.3.3 (build 12)
Installation UUID: 75a3e4df-6d6e-4364-9dac-61e6293ef1f9

issue_title: Producer cannot be garbage collected when `group.id` set.
issue_body:
I've run into an issue where my script fails to terminate when the `group.id` config option is set on a Producer. It seems to be the case that when this option is set, the Producer cannot be garbage collected (hangs forever at the point garbage collection is attempted).

Here is a minimal script that consistently reproduces it on my environment.

``` python
import logging
import sys

# Set up logging
numeric_log_level = getattr(logging, "INFO", None)
logging.basicConfig(level=numeric_log_level, stream=sys.stdout)

from confluent_kafka import Producer

config = {
    'group.id': "something-valid"
}

logging.info("Configuration: {}".format(config))

producer = Producer(config)

logging.info("Regular producer created") # This line will run

del producer # It hangs here

logging.info("Regular producer deleted") # This line is never reached
```

issue_title: Can't tell if Kafka broker is unreachable
issue_body:
I have a python app that uses the confluent Kafka client, which in turn relies on librdkafka. We are using librdkafka version 0.9.1.

I am trying to set up a Kafka producer (and later, a consumer). The actual addresses or FQDNs of the brokers is, of course, configurable. From the point of view of my app, one of the first things I am interested in before I can start my work is, did I successfully connect to a broker? I create a Producer, and I produce(), but what if the broker is down or unreachable, or the user simply entered garbage in the broker list? I find that flush() hangs for an unacceptably long time in these cases.

What I'd like is visibility and control. After I create the Producer, I want to be able to ask it, did you succeed in establishing a connection with at least one broker? I see that in the C code in librdkafka-master, there is the rd_kafka_broker_any(rk, RD_KAFKA_BROKER_STATE_UP) routine. Is there anything like this through the python API? I might want to be able to walk the broker list and ask the connection state of each. My app might want to take different actions depending on our failure to connect to the broker cluster. The closest I think I can some is a loop, asking for len(my_producer) after I produce(), and waiting for it to go fro m 1 to 0. Is there nothing better?

Is there something clever I could do with callbacks that would solve my problems?

Thanks,

-John Gregg

Here is a portion of my dummy test script:

def my_kafka_cb(err, msg):
    print("my_kafka_cb, err {}, msg {}".format(str(err), str(msg)))

conf = {'bootstrap.servers': '1.2.3.4',
        'group.id': 'mygroup',
        'debug': 'all',
        'socket.timeout.ms': 5000,
        'socket.max.fails': 1,
        'session.timeout.ms': 3000}

kafka_producer = Producer(**conf)

print("just created, len {}, about to poll 4.5".format(len(kafka_producer)))
print("************************** poll returned {}".format(kafka_producer.poll(4.5)))
kafka_producer.produce("Fred_topic", "Fred the dog went for a walk".encode('utf-8'), on_delivery=my_kafka_cb)
print("************************** just produced (len {}), about to poll 1.0".format(len(kafka_producer)))
print("************************** poll returned {}".format(kafka_producer.poll(1.0)))
kafka_producer.flush()
print("************************** after flush")

issue_title: Incorrect handling of arithmetic with folded datetimes
issue_body:
Adding a timedelta to a folded datetime produces incorrect result, see script/output. Works fine with equivalent pytz timezone.
```
import datetime
import dateutil.tz
#import pytz

d1 = datetime.datetime(2016, 11, 6, 6, 12, 15, 0, datetime.timezone.utc)
print(repr(d1))
print(d1.timestamp())
d2 = d1.astimezone(dateutil.tz.gettz('US/Eastern'))
#d2 = d1.astimezone(pytz.timezone('US/Eastern'))
print(repr(d2))
print(d2.timestamp())
d3 = d2 + datetime.timedelta(microseconds=0)
print(repr(d3))
print(d3.timestamp())

assert d1 == d2 == d3
assert d1.timestamp() == d2.timestamp() == d3.timestamp()
```

issue_title: A discussion about using `runipy` or something similar
issue_body:
To continue discussion started in #271 

@ocefpaf wrote:
> I used runipy in the past to test notebook output, but the problem, in this case, that the changes will be hard to make sense (like diffs of base64 encoded PNGs).

> I think we can do an OK job by strengthening our tests as they are and improve the HTML comparisons. I am sure that the `_repr_html_`, in that case, has a flaw that we can test.

> Or maybe (crazy idea alert) we can add runipy, generate HTMLs, publish them, and inspect them before merging a PR.

We have two levels of validation :
* Ensure that python code works without error
* Ensure that the rendered HTML+JS has the desired output

Ensuring the second level is complicated because we have to test the Javascript and the HTML rendering (and your crazy idea is interesting BTW).

But what I mean is still in the first level. We run the example notebooks, to ensure that everyone can re-run them with the master version of the code. It won't ensure that the output is ok ; but it will avoid to have example notebooks that are 2-3 version late wrt the code.

issue_title: Events when zooming
issue_body:
I'd like to draw CircleMarkers with a radius in meters (not pixels). I can compute (roughly) the # of pixels in a meter at a combination of zoom level and latitude via: 

```python
def meters_per_pixel(zoom, lat):
    """
    ground resolution = cos(latitude * pi/180) * earth circumference / map width
    via: https://gist.github.com/perrygeo/4478844
    """
    return (math.cos(lat * math.pi/180.0) * 2 * math.pi * 6378137) / (256 * 2**zoom)
```

However, since this is zoom-dependent, I'd like a way to redraw the circles when zoom level is changed. As far as I can see, there isn't an event that I can catch for this.

issue_title: Set `src_last_modified_millis` in `b2 upload_file`
issue_body:
When you use `sync` to upload files, it always sets `src_last_modified_millis`.  But uploading single files doesn't, which can cause later syncs to get confused.

I propose that `src_last_modified_millis` be set for every uploaded file.  If the user doesn't specify a value on the command line, we can take it from the file.

It would be good to check and make sure that the local clock isn't too different from the server's clock before uploading anything.

issue_title: Extend Dict field type to allow specifying type of dictionary values
issue_body:
Currently there is a Dict field type that represents raw dictionary of any data. This container field type is similar to List field type, except for List allows specifying type of list elements while Dict type does not.

Proposal: add an argument to Dict to specify type of dict values (keys are always strings). This will allow to work with mappings of some values for which number and exact list of keys is not known. Default value for this new type argument will by type Any (or Field) which does not transform/validate data which will preserve backward compatibility.

issue_title: String fields should return an error when given non-UTF-8
issue_body: Currently, if a user specifies a `bytes` value for a `String` field that is not UTF-8, marshmallow will raise a `UnicodeDecodeError`. A more sensible thing to expect from a validation library like marshmallow would be for it to return an error message about this not being a valid UTF-8 `String`.

issue_title: RFC: Update API for list_ports.comports()
issue_body:
The list of lists that is returned by ```serial.tools.list_ports.comports()``` is inflexible and prone to error.

Code and responses are from Python 3.4.3 running on OS X

For example in this invocation of ```comports()``` I get back a reply of

```python
[['/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port', 'n/a', 'n/a'],
 ['/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Modem', 'n/a', 'n/a'],
 ['/dev/cu.usbserial', 'USB-Serial Controller', 'USB VID:PID=67b:2303 SNR=None']]
```

Now I can try matching with a grep on element 2 of each possible port but this is fragile across operating systems.  On OS X we have some os specific USB properties such as location ID and if the device is considered a modem or a plain RS232 serial stream.

Location ID is a 32bit number that maps to a specific location on the USB bus that will not change if the hub topology is not changed upstream of the device.  i.e. if you leave your USB bus alone the location ID will not change.  This is useful for working with USB micro controllers that switch modes.  e.g. from a USB-CDC interface you could issue a command to your part to enter a USB-DFU mode. If you knew your location ID before, you could attach to a DFU interface at that location ID.

As different systems have different serial port metadata, I propose a list of dictionaries to be returned by ```comports()```.

the above example would become

```python
[
  {'device': '/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Incoming-Port'},
  {'device': '/dev/cu.Bluetooth-Modem'},
  {'device': '/dev/cu.usbserial',
    'USB PID': 8963,
    'USB VID': 1659, 
    'name': 'USB-Serial Controller',
    'location_id': 0xfa130000,
  },
]
```
Clearly this dictionary only includes keys that are populated. We could use the  ```collections.DefaultDict``` to default non existent keys to ```None``` for convenience.

Alternatively we could use a named tuple and make the return more static.

Function name could change to maintain compatibly.
Thoughts?

issue_title: links need fixing in docs
It is better to define constant type in AddConstant and MultConstant layer as Double rather than T
issue_body:
In order to be more user-friendly, it is better to define constant type in `AddConstant` and `MultConstant` layer as `Double` rather than `T`.

Because when user define model which including `AddConstant` or `MultConstant`, they may just `add(AddConstant[T](5.5))`, something like that, but not based on the tensor numeric type `T` like `add(AddConstant[T](ev.FromType[Double](5.5)))`

issue_title: Implementation of clearsky.detect_clearsky
issue_body:
I noticed inconsistencies between the pvlib.clearsky.detect_clearsky implementation and the reference it's based on (M. J. Reno and C. W. Hansen, “Identification of periods of clear sky irradiance in time series of GHI measurements,” Renew. Energy, vol. 90, pp. 520–531, 2016.)

The first issue is in the way slopes are calculated.  When calculating the slopes, the function implicitly assumes a data frequency of 1 minute (e.g., `meas_slope = np.diff(measured[H], n=1, axis=0)`).  Including the time differences would provide expected behavior and would reflect the equations from the original reference.  Furthermore, it may improve the performance of using the default parameters on lower frequency data.

I also believe that the calculation of the final features ("maximum difference between changes in GHI and clear sky time series" from the paper) is incorrectly calculated.  The paper shows this feature as the maximum of the absolute element-wise difference between GHI and GHI(cs) slopes for each window (eqn 14).  The implementation appears to calculate the difference between the absolute maximum GHI slope and absolute maximum GHI(cs) slope for a given window.

This isn't really a "bug", but tagging it as such seemed like the best option.  I'd be happy to resubmit the issue report under a different tag if preferred.

issue_title: Feedback requested: what version of Django REST Framework do you use?
issue_body:
How many of you are using DRF 3.0.x or 2.4.x? Since the JSON API spec just recently stabilized on 1.0 I'm guessing that most JSON API compliant APIs will be built using one of the latest versions of DRF.

If that is the case I would like to just support DRF 3.1 and newer. Django (1.6+) and Python (2.7+) support should remain unchanged.

issue_title: Handling Side-Loading Nicely
Pagination of nested relationships
issue_body:
Hello, recently started playing with this package, and while I am a bit struggling with somewhat scarce documentation, I really love the idea and how it works. 

One of the quirks I have is how to paginate nested relationships. My data can easily reach thousands of relations per object and that sometimes just hangs the server/browser.

I read the spec and [found out](http://jsonapi.org/format/#document-resource-object-relationships) that it is possible to have the server paginate also the internal relationships. I would guess that this is not implemented yet? If not, can you evaluate on how hard it would be to do something like this and maybe point me in the right direction on where to start?
