issue_title: fix numkit float/int indexing
issue_body:
The following numkit tests in `test_integration.py` fail
* Test_simps_error.test_odd_const_x 
* Test_simps_error.test_odd_const_x_dx

The problem is in `numkit.integration.simps_error()`:
```
            if len(x.shape) == 1:
                shapex = numpy.ones(nd)
                shapex[axis] = x.shape[0]
                saveshape = x.shape
                returnshape = True
               x=x.reshape(tuple(shapex))

E               TypeError: 'numpy.float64' object cannot be interpreted as an index
```
The argument for reshap (`shapex`) must be an integer or tuple of integers. 

# Fix
Probably just
```python
                shapex = numpy.ones(nd, dtype=np.int_)
```

# Background
* see https://github.com/Becksteinlab/GromacsWrapper/pull/110#issuecomment-288475330 for initial discussion and e.g. https://travis-ci.org/Becksteinlab/GromacsWrapper/jobs/187547479 for failure in numkit `test_integration`
* see rbgirshick/py-faster-rcnn#481 for description: deprecated indexing with floats has been removed in numpy 1.12.

issue_title: make MDrunner configurable
issue_body:
`MDrunner` currently only use Gromacs 4.x commands (see #64), which leads to problems in workflows ~~(eg see #63)~~.

Usage of `mdrun` and `mpiexec mdrun` (and `gmx mdrun` etc) should be configured in the cfg file (see possibly discussion at #49) and then used by `MDrunner`.

issue_title: Auto-recovery is not enabled
issue_body:
To actually have it enabled one needs to suppress any ephemeral instance storage when launching the instance.  Also, it makes sense to configure an SNS topic for AWS to deliver notifications of recovery events to.

For example, please see: https://github.com/zalando/planb-cassandra/blob/master/create_cluster.py#L375

issue_title: Timeout with regular expression

When trying the following code, it timeouts always.
I found the line that makes it timeout is ```(v_matches a!22 (v_dot (v_dot request "body") "key"))```.
There, (v_matches p1 p2) makes use of (str.in.re p2 p1).
Also, a!22 is a regular expression equivalent to ```ssh-rsa AAAA[0-9A-Za-z+/]+[=]{0,3} ([^@]+@[^@]+)```.

issue_title: (set-logic ALL) only partially disables builtin List sort
issue_body:
The following example produces an assertion failure:
```smt2
(set-logic ALL)
(declare-datatypes ((List 0)) (((Nil) (Cons (head Int) (tail List)))))
(define-fun-rec f ((n Int) (l List)) Bool
  (or (and ((_ is Nil) l))
      (and ((_ is Cons) l)
           (f (- n 1) (tail l)))))
(check-sat)
```

```
ASSERTION VIOLATION
File: /home/moritz/code/z3/src/ast/datatype_decl_plugin.cpp
Line: 39
n == get_def().params().size()
(C)ontinue, (A)bort, (S)top, (T)hrow exception, Invoke (G)DB
```

I took a look at the source and it looks like the decision on whether to create the builtin list sort is made in [`init_manager_core`](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/f22abaa7135f46953211fc304aa51e938e0d0948/src/cmd_context/cmd_context.cpp#L721). This function is apparently called twice, the first time `new_manager` is `true` and at `m_logic` is set to `ALL` so the list sort is not created. The second time `new_manager` is `false` but now `m_logic` is a null pointer so we create the list leading to the error.

Sadly, I know too little about the code to know what the right solution is or why `m_logic` is set to a null pointer the second time.

issue_title: Double->Float conversion broken
issue_body:
This used to work until a very recent builds. I suspect the fix to http://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/331 might have introduced it.

This benchmark converts to smallest possible double denormal (5.0e-324) to a single-precision value:

```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FP)
(define-fun s2 () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53) ((_ to_fp 11 53) roundNearestTiesToEven (/ 1 )))
(declare-fun s0 () (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(declare-fun s1 () (_ FloatingPoint  8 24))
(assert
   (let ((s3 (fp.eq s0 s2)))
   (let ((s5 ((_ to_fp 8 24) roundNearestTiesToEven s0)))
   (let ((s6 (fp.eq s1 s5)))
   (let ((s7 (and s3 s6)))
   s7)))))
(check-sat)
(eval s1)
```

The result should be `+0`, but Z3 says `+oo`:

```
sat
(_ +oo 8 24)
```

A similar issue exists when converting `-5.0e-324` as well.

issue_title: pyZ3 crashing
issue_body:
In ref to a previous ticket (https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/485), I've again stumbled into a situation that regularly causes python to completely crash. Here's how to reproduce:

git clone https://github.com/Owlz/pySym/tree/z3_crashing

Ensure you have z3 available for import (workon/virtualenv/etc).

Open ipython (v3)

```python
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG,format='%(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s', datefmt='%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p')
from pyPath import Path
import ast
import Colorer
from pyPathGroup import PathGroup

source = """
a = 1
"""

b = ast.parse(source).body
p = Path(b,source=source)
pg = PathGroup(p)
pg.explore()
pg.errored[0].step()
```

You can also usually replace that last step command with simply attempting to exit out of the ipython interpreter. Usually you get a general core dump message or "corrupted double-linked list: 0x00000000015d7150" type message.

issue_title: autoencoder sizes don't match
issue_body:
I am trying to train an autoencoder but without connecting the weights of the conv layer with the weights of the deconv layer. This is why I was so happy with the recent addition of the transposed layer (thanks!!!). 

Unfortunately, I can't get it to work as expected (might be my fault). Here is how I create the network:

```
from lasagne.layers import InputLayer, DenseLayer, ReshapeLayer, Upscale2DLayer, Conv2DLayer, MaxPool2DLayer, NonlinearityLayer, BiasLayer, TransposedConv2DLayer
import lasagne

class Network(object):
    def __init__(self, input_dim1, input_dim2, input_dim3):
        l_inp = InputLayer((None, input_dim1, input_dim2, input_dim3))
        l_conv1 = Conv2DLayer(l_inp, 16, 11, 3, 0, W=init.Normal(0.01),nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.sigmoid)
        l_deconv1 = TransposedConv2DLayer(l_conv1, l_conv1.input_shape[1], l_conv1.filter_size, stride = l_conv1.stride, crop = l_conv1.pad, W=init.Normal(0.01),nonlinearity=lasagne.nonlinearities.sigmoid)
        out = ReshapeLayer(l_deconv1, ([0], -1))
        self.head = out

```

I try to apply it to images of size 3x250x250 but the transposed layer ends up being of size 3x248x248. Here are the sizes of the different layers:
```
<class 'lasagne.layers.input.InputLayer'> (None, 3L, 250L, 250L)

<class 'lasagne.layers.conv.TransposedConv2DLayer'> (None, 3L, 248L, 248L)
<class 'lasagne.layers.shape.ReshapeLayer'> (None, 184512)

```
What am I doing wrong?

issue_title: Use DNN by default for convolutions
issue_body:
Since `theano.tensor.nnet.conv2d` doesn't support `same` or explicit padding, the default `Conv2DLayer` implementation of padding can be quite inefficient - even if cuDNN is installed and Theano is using it under the hood. 

I think we should substitute `Conv2DDNNLayer` for `Conv2DLayer` automatically whenever possible.

issue_title: Update recurrent layer module docstring
issue_body:
The [module docstring for `lasagne.layers.recurrent`](http://lasagne.readthedocs.io/en/latest/modules/layers/recurrent.html) has an example that uses reshapes to put a dense layer on top of a recurrent layer -- this should be updated to use the `num_leading_axes` argument of the `DenseLayer` instead.
In addition, it would be good to have a second example that uses a Conv1D layer to preprocess a sequence fed into an RNN, to highlight (a) that this is possible and (b) that you'll need a DimshuffleLayer in between (see https://groups.google.com/d/msg/lasagne-users/A5UCHKxo_Ys/GO2MV6XuDAAJ).

issue_title: Generalize ElemwiseSumLayer
issue_body:
I need a multiplicative merge layer instead of sum. What are the thoughts about generalizing ElemwiseSumLayer to something like `ElemwiseMergeLayer` and let the user decide what merge function to use.

`ElemwiseMergeLayer` could be identical to `ElemwiseSumLayer` except that should take an additional merge_fun parameter.

issue_title: Add NWBRefSpec class
issue_body:
## 2) Feature Request

For consistency, it would be good to add a ``NWBRefSpec` ` class just so that all the spec classes from form have a counterpart in ``pynwb.spec``.  Currently this class would not need to define Iany custom behavior so it would be just a primitive class:

```
import pynwb.form.spec.spec.RefSpec

class NWBRefSpec(RefSpec):
    pass
```

### Problem/Use Case

The primary use-case is to simplify and improve consistency of imports needed when creating extensions to enable that all spec classes can be imported directly from ``pynwb.spec``.

## Checklist

- [X ] Have you ensured the feature or change was not already [reported](https://github.com/NeurodataWithoutBorders/pynwb/issues/) ?
- [X] Have you included a brief and descriptive title?
- [X] Have you included a clear description of the problem you are trying to solve?
- [X] Have you included a minimal code snippet that reproduces the issue you are encountering?

issue_title: __float__ method makes numpy/pandas forget currency
issue_body:
```
>>> pandas.DataFrame([moneyed.Money()]).sum()
0    0.00 XYZ
dtype: object

>>> pandas.DataFrame([djmoney.money.Money()]).sum()
0    0.0  # <-- currency missing
dtype: float64
```

This is due to implementing the `__float__` method:

```
class MM(moneyed.Money):
    def __float__(self):
        return float(self.amount)
```

>>> pandas.DataFrame([MM()]).sum()

issue_title: [jext] Automatically reconnect on websocket disconnect
issue_body:
This is twofold:

* If the websocket gets disconnected for any reason, we should reconnect (assuming it wasn't because we asked the kernel to be killed)
* Evaluate if we're still connected to the server by polling an endpoint on the notebook server. We can provide an `/api/ping` as necessary

issue_title: Add a theme for visually-disabled users
issue_body:
This starts us off thinking about accessibility for the notebook, something that we should tackle early on.

The easiest issue to take on is accessibility for visually-impaired users. We'll need to create a new theme for the application (ala `static/styles/light.css`) that includes some of the following modifications.
- High contrast text-background color combination (for example, yellow text on a black background)
- Larger font-sizes on all elements
- Test how nteract behaves with screen magnifiers and identify possible bugs

issue_title: If-Match: * does not match the spec
issue_body: Per https://github.com/Kinto/kinto/pull/1064#issuecomment-279351031, the behavior of `If-Match: *` is the opposite of `If-None-Match: *`. `If-None-Match: *` should succeed if no resource exists at the given URL and fail if any resource exists at the given URL, but `If-Match: *` should fail if no resource exists at the given URL and succeed if any resource exists at the given URL. Currently `If-Match: *` does nothing (it always succeeds).

issue_title: Feature Request: Discover by default ports
issue_body:
I don't know if it has been discussed before, but I had the idea of discovering services by the ports they use by default. Is this a thing netdisco should do? The basic concept of what I mean:

```python
#!/usr/bin/python3
import asyncio
import time

NOW = time.time()
DESTS = ["192.168.1.{}".format(i) for i in range(1, 255)]
PORTS = {
    1883: "mqtt",
    2000: "homematic-wired",
    2001: "homematic-wireless",
    2010: "homematic-ip",
    3306: "mysql",
    5432: "postgres",
    6466: "androidtv",
    8086: "influxdb",
    8883: "mqtts",
    # etc.
}

async def check_port(ip, port, loop):
    try:
        reader, writer = await asyncio.open_connection(ip, port, loop=loop)
        print(ip, port, PORTS[port])
        writer.close()
        return (ip, port, True)
    except:
        return (ip, port, False)

async def run(dests, ports, loop):
    tasks = [asyncio.ensure_future(check_port(d, p, loop)) for d in dests for p in ports]
    responses = await asyncio.gather(*tasks)
    return responses

LOOP = asyncio.get_event_loop()
FUTURE = asyncio.ensure_future(run(DESTS, PORTS.keys(), LOOP))
LOOP.run_until_complete(FUTURE)
print('#'*50)
print('Total time: ', time.time() - NOW)
```
(modified version of [this](http://gunhanoral.com/python/2017/07/04/async-port-check.html))

It takes me ~130 seconds to scan my class C network, but the main relevant services actually show up rather quickly. I wouldn't go as far as expecting the results to be 100% accurate. But maybe services discovered this way could show a proposed HASS component (with link to documentation) that might work together with the discovered service.

That way especially newbies could be informed, that they have something that might be integrated into HASS, which they have not thought before because they haven't looked through all the components HASS supports. I would consider this a workaround for services which don't publish themselves via mDNS etc.

The question would be: do you think netdisco would be improved by using this approach?

issue_title: Multi Livy Support
Make IPython version requirement less restrictive
issue_body:
Right now we're pinning to IPython v4.0.2, which is inconvenient for users running other IPython versions.  4.0.2 isn't even the most recent IPython, [4.1.1 is](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ipython/4.1.1).

We should make this a less strict inequality so that we can accept any version of IPython >= 4.0.2 (for example).

issue_title: Use webui components into another website
issue_body:
We can use webui components (command list, host list, livestate list, minemap...) on a website.

For that, we need:
* Pass the backend token in auth basic to webui call
* be able to ajax display a part of webui (like command list without headers, footers and menu)

issue_title: Multicam not possible in Unity wrapper through RealSenseDevice.cs script ?
issue_body:
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D435                                                       | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.09.11.00                                             | 
| Operating System & Version |  Win 10                                                   | 
| Platform                                 | PC                                                          |
| SDK Version                            |   2.11.1                                                   | 
| Language                               |  C/C#/unity                                              | 

### Issue Description

Hi community,
i'm trying to modify the Unity wrapper project (scene : _colored point cloud.unity_) to show 2 or more point clouds from 2 or more depth cams :

The _RealSenseDevice.cs_ script has a **RequestSerialNumber** field which seems to be made for that, and indeed, when i change this serial i can choose another cam. BUT, when i duplicate the _RealsenseDevice_ GameObject in the editor to get two different cams with different serials, the same cam is used to generate two point clouds (please note that i had to tweak the script a little to make this work because it doesn't allow multiple instances in its original shape, but this is not the subject). And in fact, we can see in the editor in the _RealSense Device Inspector_ that the same serial number is found by the two GameObjects.

After investigations in the code, i see that this function is called : _Config.EnableDevice(RequestedSerialNumber)_ which wraps this realsense c++ function : _rs2_config_enable_device_. Furthermore, in the header comments of this function we can read 

> Select a specific device explicitly by its serial number, to be used by the pipeline...

  (see [here](https://github.com/IntelRealSense/librealsense/blob/v2.8.0/include/librealsense2/h/rs_pipeline.h#L231) )

So i'm confused...
Does it speak to someone?

issue_title: xioctl(VIDIOC_S_FMT) failed Last Error: Device or resource busy
issue_body:
Camera mode: RS415
OS: Ubuntu 16.04 64-bit
Language: Using librealsense python wrapper for data acquisition and processing.

I am trying to debug my Python code that is retrieving point cloud information from realsense D415 sensor. The sensor usually works fine but during debug if I have to quit debugging and restart the debug process it says:

`xioctl(VIDIOC_S_FMT) failed Last Error: Device or resource busy`
`Process finished with exit code 0`

For proprietary issues I cannot put my code here but essentially it's acquiring x,y,z information from the sensor and computing various things on that data.

This usually happens during debugging the code. Has anyone faced similar issue before? 

Any help is appreciated. Please let me know if you need further information.

issue_title: SR300 infrared projector doesn't switch off if too close object
issue_body:
Hello,
I have a problem to set the power of the infrared projector of my SR300 (firmware 3.15), I discovered that if an object is too close, the projector stays on even if I set to 0.
Here is a video of my experiment : https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=knnLdWL8SXg
I use a realsense SR300, taking only the color and infrared flux, depth disabled.
rsCamera->enable_stream(rs::stream::infrared, 640, 480, rs::format::y8, 30);
rsCamera->enable_stream(rs::stream::color, 1280, 720, rs::format::rgb8, 10);
The code just loop and variate the laser power with set_option(rs::option::f200_laser_power, power) and print the state and the power value on the screen (the state "off" means we set the value to 0)
I observed that if an object is too close (roughly below 60cm) to the camera at initialisation, the laser power stays fixed and doesn't change value until I liberate the area. After I removed the object, I can put back in place, everything will work fine, but I have no guarantee it will be the case in my scenario.

issue_title: Error in RealSense Viewer after plugging in D435
issue_body:
Hi there

I got my D435 around a month ago. It used to work fine on my (Windows) computer when using it via the Viewer.

Hadn't touched it in awhile and upgraded to SDK 2.0 latest version (2.10.0) to test out the new functionality. Upon plugging the device in, the Viewer always gives the following error:

`Backend in rs2_create_device(info_list:000001B8F1C39EE0, index:0):
_source->QueryInterface(__uuidof(IKsTopologyInfo), reinterpret_cast<void **>(&ks_topology_info)) returned: HResult 0x80004002: "No such interface supported"`

I have tried using our other D435 on this computer, same error. I tried the same SDK version installed on another computer and it works fine, so it's something on my computer only. I also tried installing the old version (2.9.1) on my computer and it also had the same error now, so it must have been something that changed on my computer recently. Changing USB ports did not help.

The main things I remember changing here include installing the most recent Cuda version. Could this affect it at all?

Any help is appreciated. Thanks.

issue_title: Can't build Dynamic Calibration API samples
issue_body:
Hi,

I installed the latest calibration API from here: https://downloadcenter.intel.com/download/27415/Intel-RealSense-D400-Series-Dynamic-Calibration-Software-Tools (v2.5.2.0 at present), but when I try to open `CalibrationToolAPI\2.5.2.0\examples\DynamicCalibrator\RS400DynamicCalibrationSample.sln` in VS17 and build the solution it fails with 11 "cannot open include file" errors.

Is the source code available for DSDynamicCalibrationAPI.dll please? I'm currently trying to calibrate a camera pair using OpenCV/Matlab and finding the results from those differ from the Librealsense extrinsics so wanted to see how the LRS calibration works to see if I can transform my results to get it working.

I'm also wondering if it would be possible to modify the DynamicCalibrator app to obtain an extrinsic calibration between a RealSense camera and an external (rigidly connected) camera? I can see that the `ListCameras` function used in `Main.cpp` filters out non RS400 cameras; is there a hard limit on the cameras which can be used due to hardware constraints? I'm not asking you to do this for me; I'm happy to have a crack at it myself (though I may have questions!), just wanted an expert opinion on its plausibility before going to far in to it.

Thanks!

issue_title: Python 3 compatibility
issue_body:
Is Python 3 compatibility planned at this time (or any, for that matter)? If so, do you want to keep the project both Python 2 and Python 3 compatible?

It's really easy to get the examples running on Python 3 currently, it just requires dropping the `wsgiref` in `requirements.txt` and swapping the `xrange` calls.

issue_title: Does this package support Python 3?
issue_body:
On 19 February 2017, Python 3 will be 3,000 days old!  We are interested to see if we can get **at least 50%** of the [Top 5,000 PyPI packages](http://python3wos.mybluemix.net/5000) to compatible with Python 3 by that date. issue_title: Allow writing a resolver that returns a SQLAlchemy as a relay connection
issue_body:
I would like to return a relay connection for queries. Using standard graphene-sqlalchemy you can do this:

```python
class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    node = relay.Node.Field()

    all_users = SQLAlchemyConnectionField(User)
```

For various places where you need a more custom search the simplest approach is a custom resolver:

```python
class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    node = relay.Node.Field()

    all_users = graphene.List(User)

    def resolve_all_users(self, info, **args):
        # Grab the root SQLAlchemy query
        query = User.get_query(info)
        return query.filter(
            User.active,
            # and possibly a whole bunch of other filters)
        ).options(joinedload(User.emails).all()
```

This works, but it returns a plain list of users without any support for pagination or other relay connection tricks handled by SQLAlchemyConnectionField. What I am looking for is a way to return a query, and have it automatically handled in a similar way as SQLAlchemyConnectionField does. Perhaps by introducing something like a `query_to_relay_connection` function you can use like this:

```python
class Query(graphene.ObjectType):
    node = relay.Node.Field()

    all_users = graphene.List(User)

    def resolve_all_users(self, info, **args):
        # Grab the root SQLAlchemy query
        query = User.get_query(info)
        query = query.filter(
            User.active,
            # and possibly a whole bunch of other filters)
        ).options(joinedload(User.emails)
        return query_to_relay_connection(query, User)
```

this can probably reuse a lot of code SQLAlchemyConnectionField currently uses internally.

issue_title: Capture and process salt state return info to output failed states in traceback
issue_body: When a state fails, watchmaker throws a traceback. That traceback is often viewed as and reported as the error message, but it is just indicative of a failed state. The actual failure is in the debug log output, earlier in the log stream. The change proposed here would be to capture the return info of the salt state output and process it, searching for failed states and reporting them in the traceback, to improve the message in the log.

issue_title: Multi-threading on multiple epochs
issue_body:
The line 253 in fastext.cc receives one line from the stream at a time:

`localTokenCount += dict_->getLine(ifs, line, labels, model.rng);`

The different parts of the `ifs` are correctly distributed among the threads in the beginning (line 238 in `trainThread`). For one epoch I think it is OK, but when we do multiple epochs, the stream is being set to the beginning in line 258 of dictionary.cc when it reaches the end. If my reasoning is correct, this will lead to all the threads reading the same data (beginning of the file) in second epoch, instead of each thread reading a different part of the file.

issue_title: Driver should take host of nodes, not specific API path
issue_labels: [{'id': 430255243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/bigchaindb/bigchaindb-driver/labels/topic:%20UX%20docs%20cmd%20api%20driver', 'name': 'topic: UX docs cmd api driver', 'color': '1d76ff', 'default': False}, {'id': 426414349, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/bigchaindb/bigchaindb-driver/labels/z:%20dummy-label-for-waffle', 'name': 'z: dummy-label-for-waffle', 'color': 'cccccc', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Currently the driver class takes any URI as a valid parameter for a URI pointing to a BDB node:
See: https://github.com/bigchaindb/bigchaindb-driver/blob/master/bigchaindb_driver/driver.py#L34

When doing requests, the connection's `path` just specifies the path on a single level (e.g. `/transactions`), so that when initializing the driver with:

`Driver('http://ipdb.foundation', 'http://localhost:9984')`

requests fail, as for both specified URIS, their path to the API + VERSION is missing.
To fix the earlier given example:

`Driver('http://ipdb.foundation/api/v1', 'http://localhost:9984/api/v1')`.

This ticket suggests to add the paths in the initialization method of the driver to the node-URI given by the user.

issue_title: csvstat chokes on "inf" numbers
issue_body:
For the following input CSV (yes, 1 row, 1 column, 1 header):

    inf

`csvstat` (no other argument besides input.csv) outputs:

```

	Contains null values:  False
	Unique values:         1
	Most common values:    [<class 'decimal.InvalidOperation'>]
```

This happens because `python-babel` (in Debian system 2.4.0) number formatting function chokes on Infinity, e.g.:
```
from decimal import Decimal
from babel.numbers import format_decimal
format_decimal(Decimal("Infinity"))
```

`format_decimal` is called from `csvstat.py:269` (in Debian system 1.0.2-1).

Interestingly `csvstat --freq` outputs:

{ "Infinity": 1 }
```

issue_title: Fail to validate HTTPS server while using custom CA file
issue_body:
Hello httplib2.
I am using httplib2==0.9.2.

My use case is quite simple: we have a private network and have setup some HTTPS services. These services provide certificates signed by a site-local CA authority. All certificates are signed using SHA-256 as signing algorithm.

Take that: 
- a self-signed root CA certificate exists inside `/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt`  (the standard debian location)
- the certificate for CN `foo.localdomain` is signed by this local authority, and is installed to a web server https://foo.localdomain

I'am trying to use httplib2 to communicate with this service: 

``` python
h = httplib2.Http(ca_certs='/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt')
resp, cont = h.request('https://foo.localdomain')
```

The above fails to perform a proper handshake, but unfortunately it returns poor feedback on where the actual problem is:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "example.py", line 3, in <module>
    resp, cont = h.request('https://foo.localdomain')
  File "/home/malex/pyenv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1609, in request
    (response, content) = self._request(conn, authority, uri, request_uri, method, body, headers, redirections, cachekey)
  File "/home/malex/pyenv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1351, in _request
    (response, content) = self._conn_request(conn, request_uri, method, body, headers)
  File "/home/malex/pyenv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1272, in _conn_request
    conn.connect()
  File "/home/malex/pyenv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/httplib2/__init__.py", line 1059, in connect
    raise SSLHandshakeError(e)
httplib2.SSLHandshakeError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed (_ssl.c:581)
```

At the same time, the following `s_client` test succeeds (certificate verification returns `0`):

```
openssl s_client -connect foo.localdomain:443 -servername foo.localdomain -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
```

Also, `curl` succeeds:

```
curl --cacert /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt https://foo.localdomain

issue_title: pybind's CMake script finds version 3.5 although PYBIND11_PYTHON_VERSION was set to 2.7
issue_body:
I want to built a python module with pybind11 and CMake. At the moment Python 2.7 should be used.
I'm on Kubuntu 16.04, CMake 3.6, pybind11 2.0.0.

The CMake snippet I use is:
```
set(PYBIND11_PYTHON_VERSION 2.7)
add_subdirectory(../../pybind11-2.0.0 ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/pybind11)
[...]
pybind11_add_module([...])
[...]
```
Note, that pybind is not a subdirectory, but located somewhere else in the repo.
In the CMake output I get something like:
```
-- Found PythonInterp: /usr/bin/python3.5 (found version "3.5.2") 
-- Found PythonLibs: /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpython3.5m.so
```
Python 3.5 is used even if I set `PYBIND11_PYTHON_VERSION` to 2.7.

I digged into the CMake files of pybind11 and found the following in `pybind11Tools.cmake`:
```
set(Python_ADDITIONAL_VERSIONS 3.7 3.6 3.5 3.4)
find_package(PythonLibsNew ${PYBIND11_PYTHON_VERSION} REQUIRED)
```
And in `FindPythonLibsNew.cmake`:
```
if(PythonLibsNew_FIND_REQUIRED)
    find_package(PythonInterp ${PythonLibsNew_FIND_VERSION} REQUIRED)
else()
    find_package(PythonInterp ${PythonLibsNew_FIND_VERSION})
endif()
```
The docs to [FindPythonInterp](https://cmake.org/cmake/help/v3.6/module/FindPythonInterp.html) states, that "The Python_ADDITIONAL_VERSIONS variable can be used to specify a list of version numbers that should be taken into account when searching for Python".
Is pybind11 2.0.0 only for Python version 3.4 and above?

issue_title: Member raw pointer assignment
issue_body:
I am trying (as an example) to build a wrapper for Zlib, which has the following (simplified) structure:

```cpp
struct ZStream{
    char* input;
};

namespace py = pybind11;
PYBIND11_PLUGIN(example) {
    py::module m("example", "pybind11 example plugin");
     
    py::class_<ZStream>(m, "ZStream")
        .def(py::init<>())
        .def_readwrite("input", &ZStream::input);
```

This fails with message: 
```bash
include\pybind11\cast.h(581): error C2664: 'void pybind11::class_< ZStream,std::unique_ptr<type,std::default_delete<_Ty>>>::def_readwrite::<lambda_0efac106066633b4fb2e3c6d9eedfded>::operator ()(C &,const D &) const': cannot convert argument
2 from 'char' to 'char *const &' [C:\Users\drodri\workspace\accu\accu\5pybind11\build\example.vcxproj]
          with
          [
              type=ZStream,
              _Ty=ZStream,
              C=ZStream,
              D=char *
          ]
  c:\users\drodri\.conan\data\pybind11\1.4\memsharded\stable\package\5ab84d6acfe1f23c4fae0ab88f26e3a396351ac9\include\pybind11\cast.h(581): note: Reason: cannot convert from
  'char' to 'char *const '
```

I have tried casting "input", but without success.
It builds if I use ``def_readonly``, but then fails at run time, as the field is not-assignable (which makes sense I guess).

I am able to do it with ctypes, this works for my use case:

```python
class _z_stream(ctypes.Structure):
    _fields_ = [
        ("next_in", ctypes.POINTER(ctypes.c_ubyte)),
        ... 
    ]

def compress(input, level=6):
    st = _z_stream()
    ...
    st.next_in   = ctypes.cast(ctypes.c_char_p(input), ctypes.POINTER(ctypes.c_ubyte))
```

I cannot find in the docs or examples how to handle this. Is it possible to manage such low level pointer assignment? I am not willing to modify the original source code to add setters/getters, as it the ZLib, would like to keep it as-is.
Thanks very much.

issue_title: Problem with constructor for a class with custom operator new
Returning std::vector of move-only holders
issue_body:
The `const holder &` entries in an `std::vector` (and supposedly also other STL containers) cannot be passed to `move_only_holder_caster::cast(holder_type &&src, return_value_policy, handle)`.

(Apparently, for custom non-holder move-only types, this is ok, since the `return_value_policy` is forced to be `move` and the overload of `type_caster::cast(const type &, return_value_policy, handle)` creates a move_constructor that casts away the constness.)

Example code:
```C++
py::class_<A> classA(m, "A");
m.def("test", [] () { return std::vector<std::unique_ptr<A>>(); });
```

issue_title: How to test if a key exists in py::dict
PYBIND11_CPP_STANDARD detects and adds the /std:c++14 flag when CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17
issue_body:
Hi!

Sorry for the (maybe) bad title, it is hard to summarise this in one line.

I'm using the latest VS 2017 Preview (15.3.0 Preview 7.1) with the latest CMake nightly (cmake-3.9.20170812-g71ee6).

CMake has recently added support for `set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17)` on MSVC, which will set `/std:c++17` (in fact it sets `-std:c++17` which still works but I guess they should use the slash instead so that the VS IDE displays it correctly) (https://gitlab.kitware.com/cmake/cmake/issues/16482).

Now pybind11 detects `PYBIND11_CPP_STANDARD` to be `/std:c++14`, and adds the `/std:c++14` flag in addition to `/std:c++17` which is added by CMake, resulting in a (benign) compiler warning:

> cl : Command line warning D9025: overriding '/std:c++14' with '/std:c++17'

I realise I'm a bit cutting-edge here but both of these things will be released officially soon so we may well improve them now or at least have it on the radar. 
I think the logic here (https://github.com/pybind/pybind11/blob/master/tools/pybind11Tools.cmake#L35) will need some adjustments. I'm happy to try to change it if you give me some guidance, or test a fix.

Cheers!

issue_title: "Invalid XML encountered": control characters in XML
issue_labels: [{'id': 89425274, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/cat:%20server%20compat', 'name': 'cat: server compat', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': False}, {'id': 336203794, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/in%20progress', 'name': 'in progress', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}, {'id': 292790365, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/lv:%20easy', 'name': 'lv: easy', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 336204756, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/ready', 'name': 'ready', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}, {'id': 100972838, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/type:%203rd%20party', 'name': 'type: 3rd party', 'color': 'd70a53', 'default': False}, {'id': 80748618, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/pimutils/vdirsyncer/labels/type:%20enhancement', 'name': 'type: enhancement', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
* vdirsyncer version: 0.15.0
* server: owncloud 8.2.2 (has sabre 2.1.7)
* Python version: 3.6
* Your operating system: openbsd 6.1
* Your config file: not applicable

So I got this error: 
error: The server returned something vdirsyncer doesn't understand. Error message: InvalidXMLResponse('Invalid XML encountered: not well-formed (invalid token): line 430, column 21\nDouble-check the URLs in your config.',)

Running --verbosity=DEBUG and looking at this line in the returned XML, I find this (censored) event: 
```
<cal:calendar-data>BEGIN:VCALENDAR 
debug: VERSION:2.0                                                                             
debug: PRODID:ownCloud Calendar                                                                
 debug: CALSCALE:GREGORIAN                                                                      
 debug: BEGIN:VEVENT                                                                            
 debug: UID:<censored>                                                                          
 debug: DTSTAMP:<censored>                                                              
 debug: CREATED:<censored>                                                                     
 debug: LAST-MODIFIED:<censored>                                                               
 debug: SUMMARY:<censored>                                                            
 debug: DTSTART;TZID=<censored>                                                   
 debug: DTEND;TZID=<censored>                                                     
 debug: LOCATION:                                                                               
 debug: DESCRIPTION:<censored>^D<censored>                                  
 debug: CATEGORIES:                                                                             
 debug: END:VEVENT                                                                              
 debug: END:VCALENDAR                                                                           
 debug: </cal:calendar-data>
```
A stray ^D char in the DESCRIPTION field (how did it get there?). is returned. Other clients (owncloud web client, thunderbird lightning) don't throw an error at this. 

Running sync again...:
`error: The server returned something vdirsyncer doesn't understand. Error message: InvalidXMLResponse('Invalid XML encountered: not well-formed (invalid token): line 1189, column 21\nDouble-check the URLs in your config.',)`

Now what? 
```
...
1189: debug: SUMMARY:<censored>^O
...
```
Ugh! And not only 1189, but a lot of other events had stray ^Ds and ^Os. Turns out copying text from pdfs leaks control characters in your calendar, who knew. Removing those characters from the events allowed me to finally sync.

issue_title: Initiating a Direct transfer gets stuck when partner is offline
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

If we have a channel with a partner and attempt a (Direct) transfer while he is offline then the API call will get stuck.


## Drawbacks

The counterparty may be malicious, and actually just withhold the ACK, while he already has sent us the transfer.

## Solution

This needs thinking:

End the API call after a timeout and provide an error message to the user if we don't get the `ACK` on time. But we have to find a way to handle the drawback of a malicious partner pretending to be offline.

The most direct way to handle this would be to end the attempted transfer after the timeout because we have not received an ACK, provide an error message to the user and then immediately close the channel and go in the blockchain since any transfers from that point could be unsafe.

issue_title: Update pyethereum dependency to upstream version 1.6.1
Block raiden startup until the ethereum client is fully synchronized
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Raiden may be connected to an ethereum client that is not fully synchronized, under this circumstance the ethereum client will not provided the latest event from the channel the node cares and would effectively make Raiden to be unaware of channel changes.

## Solution

Wait for the geth node to fully synchronize before starting to operate.

This is only a proper solution if the raiden node is starting fresh, i.e. the first run without pending transfers, and it's only as a tool to prevent bad behavior, it's not sufficient for scenarios were the ethereum client had it's database deleted, we cannot prevent that from happening and there is not way to recover aside from wait until the database is synchronized, for this scenario third parties are required to provide safe operation and are outside of the scope of this issue.

## Tasklist

- [ ] On startup query the ethereum node and hold until it's synchronized.

issue_title: define the structure of JSON-encoded objects (for the API)
issue_body:
Related to #10 

*The structure of the JSON-encoding of objects has implications on how a client has to deserialise them again. Therefore a logical structure has to be thought of.*

**The current proposal**:

see PR [280](https://github.com/ezdac/raiden/blob/websocket_server/raiden/api/encoding.py#L51)

every object will be enveloped with information on what type of object the JSON-string represents and how to decode it (in terms of an API-version specifier):

For example an ChannelSettled - Event will look like this:

```python
 {
            'type': 'Event',
            'classifier': 'channel_settled',
            'version': '0.9b',
            'data': {
                'netting_channel_address': '0x624c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                'block_number': '1'
            },
 }
```
- the `type` specifies the Base-class of the Object `Event`, `Channel`, ...
- the `classifier` specifies the Sub-Class of the Object `channel_settled`, `channel_new`, `null`
- the `version` specifies the API-version that encoded the object
- `data` will contain the nested, encoded Object itself


There will also be a `List` type, that represents some flat list of objects, whose base-class (former: `type`) is specified in the `classifier`

```python
 {
            'type': 'List',
            'classifier': 'channel',
            'version': '0.9b',
            'data': [{
                    'netting_channel_address': '0x624c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'partner_address': '0x524c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'asset_address': '0x424c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'balance': 42,
                    'block_number': '1',
                    'status': 'closed'
                },
                {
                    'netting_channel_address': '0x324c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'partner_address': '0x724c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'asset_address': '0x425c4a42215d63282b5e3f754d7b3d5e542c3842',
                    'balance': 42,
                    'block_number': '4',
                    'status': 'open'
                }
            ]
 }
```

Especially for events, this List has to support polymorphic fields, since there will be differently structured Events appended. Here, following the proposed schema, we would also have to envelope every Event in the list.

The proposed implementation of encoding uses for the most part the `webargs` [module](https://github.com/sloria/webargs),
which heavily wraps around the `marshmallow` [module](https://marshmallow.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)

### To Do:
- please give some feedback on the encoding schema
- especially think about how objects should be enveloped in regard to flat lists that represent arbitrary collections of objects

### To Do *implementation wise*:
- construct a custom `marshmallow.field.Field` for polymorphic fields or use the `marshmallow-polyfield` [extension](https://github.com/Bachmann1234/marshmallow-polyfield)

issue_title: Cluster job submission and monitoring features.
issue_body:
Given that it is relatively troublesome to set up and use rq/celery task queues (dedicated redis server and workers are needed), we should design a new "cluster" queue to facilitate the use of traditional clusters with PBS/Torch/SunGrid systems. The following is a short description of the features that will need to be fully developed and implemented.

1. **`task` statement**: the task directive will be responsible for sending jobs to task queues and clusters. It is chosen because we have a mechanism already to send necessary environmental information to the tasks and make them self-contained jobs. 

2.  **job parameters** such as memory, node, walltime etc should be acceptable by `task` statement. (issue #240). These parameters will be backend independent because all queue systems need them.

3. **how to transfer files** this is related to #257 . Basically if the cluster and desktop are on different filesystems, we will need a way to automatically transfer input files to cluster and transfer output files back. Interface wise we can
    
    * define global file transfer commands in *config.yml*. e.g.
        `sos config --set to_cluster 'rsync -av {} mycluster:/path/project/`
        `sos config --set from_cluster 'rsync -av mycluster:/path/project/{} {}`

    * automatic file transfer. SoS will transfer all input files automatically to the cluster before execution, and transfer all output files back after execution. 

    * explicit file transfer: users can specify files and directories to be transferred. This can possibly be achieved by parameter `shared` of `task`.

    * Note that snakemake uses [remote files](https://snakemake.readthedocs.io/en/latest/snakefiles/remote_files.html) although it requires users to run snakemake commands on the cluster (which is discouraged if run on head node).

4. **how to submit jobs** Not sure how to do this, but the easiest method seems to be defining a command to remotely submit a jobs (e.g. `ssh mycluster:...`). The key part is that we should get a handle of "job" (job id) for query purpose.

4. **how to monitor jobs** Not sure how to do this but we could potentially query the status with job id using commands. Note that there can be separate web or command line monitoring interface for clusters.

5. **how to wait for the completion of jobs in batch mode** The step will simply wait for the completion of the job (signature written).

6. **how to wait for the completion of jobs in Jupyter notebook**. The front-end can collect the jobs that are running and display widgets to indicate status of the jobs.

7. **prevention of duplicated jobs**. Each job will have a remote signature and a lock so duplicated jobs will be prevented (the same job id would be returned).

8. **A sos daemon**? It might worth the trouble to run a sos daemon to accept, submit and monitor jobs.

9. **cluster-specific specification**. We should use `sos config` to define cluster specific parameters. public-key ssh access would be required to avoid storing passwords.

10. **queue configuration** We should make use of existing parameter `--queue` to specify queues to use. Once we have PBS working (`-q pbs`), it should be trivial to support additional systems such as `torch`, `moab` etc.

We should first investigate other technologies such as [ganglia](http://ganglia.info/), snakemake,  [drmaa-python](http://drmaa-python.github.io/), and [pbs-drmaa](http://apps.man.poznan.pl/trac/pbs-drmaa).

Here is an interesting read on [bioinformatics workflow using Galaxy + Makeflow](http://ccl.cse.nd.edu/research/papers/scaling-escience-2015.pdf)

issue_title: Option `-r` (resume) for command sos run
issue_body:
With the no-wait mode, workflows in the following style

```
[1]
task:

[2]
task:
```

need to be executed a few times to complete

```
sos run script   # submit task in step 1
sos run script  # rerun step 1, continue to step 2 if task in step 1 is completed.
```

Option `-s force` does not play well with the no-wait mode though, because
```
sos run script -s force
```
will always re-run tasks in step 1.

Another problem is that

```
sos run script
```

will always run the workflow until it reaches the tasks that was last submitted. It can be better to check the status of the pending tasks directly, and quit is none of the tasks have been completed.

I therefore propose the addition of option `-r` that

1. sos records the list of pending jobs when it quits in no-wait mode.
2. When starting with `-r`, it checks the tasks and quit if none of the tasks has been completed.
3. sos cannot yet resume directly from the middle of execution but we can certainly try that so that `-r` can directly resume from saved point.

The existing `-r` option is for `report` which I can remove without hesitation.

issue_title: Smart handeling of pipeline timeouts
issue_body:
I am currently working on a problem where i'm optimizing a pipeline quite a large dataset (100,000 obs, 80 features). A lot of the pipelines (approx 20/100) time out with the time limit on 15 minutes, which is basically a waste of computation time. Would there be any way to avoid such cases?

Possibilities: 
- Keep counts of timeout of each operator, and disable a operator during the optimization when it has caused a number of timeouts. 
- Limit the 'config' a priori, depending on the timeout parameters, based on rules of thump or metamodels.

issue_title: Special token index guarantee.
issue_body:
**Context**
Special tokens are frequently used for masking or padding or interpreting the model. It's important in a Encoder/Decoder context that the decoder and encoder share the same indexes for EOS, SOS, and PAD. 

**Problem**
Creating two fields, one for French and one for English, there are no class constants for the index of `eos_token`. The only way to find out the index of `eos_token` is per instance of the class (etc. `self.stoi[eos_token]`).

The code by default is not designed to guarantee that the French dictionary has the same EOS index as the English dictionary.

**Possible Solution A**
With setting the optional parameter 'eos_token' would it be possible to set 'eos_token_index'?

**Possible Solution B**
Vocab or Field constant for the index of special tokens.

issue_title: ascii vs. utf-8 in torchtext/datasets/translation.py
issue_body:
@nelson-liu: I incorrectly brought this up in pull #52, new issue here

When trying to load splits for IWSLT (in french, german, etc...), the loading process would fail with an ascii encoding/decoding error:
```
.data/iwslt/de-en/IWSLT16.TED.dev2010.de-en.en.xml
.data/iwslt/de-en/IWSLT16.TED.tst2013.de-en.de.xml
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "test.py", line 25, in <module>
    train, val, test = datasets.IWSLT.splits(exts=('.de', '.en'), fields=(DE, EN))
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/torchtext/datasets/translation.py", line 116, in splits
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/torchtext/datasets/translation.py", line 136, in clean
UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode character u'\xe4' in position 60: ordinal not in range(128)
```
These are my library versions:
```
numpy==1.13.3
regex==2017.9.23
spacy==1.9.0
torch==0.2.0.post4
torchtext==0.2.0b0 (just cloned a few minutes before error)
torchvision==0.1.9
```
Here is the code that I was using, from test/translation.py:
```
from torchtext import data
from torchtext import datasets

import re
import spacy
import sys

spacy_de = spacy.load('de')
spacy_en = spacy.load('en')

url = re.compile('(<url>.*</url>)')

def tokenize_de(text):
    return [tok.text for tok in spacy_de.tokenizer(url.sub('@URL@', text))]

def tokenize_en(text):
    return [tok.text for tok in spacy_en.tokenizer(url.sub('@URL@', text))]

# Testing IWSLT
DE = data.Field(tokenize=tokenize_de)
EN = data.Field(tokenize=tokenize_en)
train, val, test = datasets.IWSLT.splits(exts=('.de', '.en'), fields=(DE, EN))
```
The following fixed it for me, in torchtext/datasets/translation.py. Replace opens with io.opens specifying utf-8 for python2. It's worth noting that a friend with python3 did not have this problem.
```
127     @staticmethod
128     def clean(path):
129         for f_xml in glob.iglob(os.path.join(path, '*.xml')):
130             print(f_xml)
131             f_txt = os.path.splitext(f_xml)[0]
132             import io
133             with io.open(f_txt, mode="w", encoding="utf-8") as fd_txt: <--- INSERT
134             #with open(f_txt, 'w') as fd_txt: <--- COMMENT
135                 root = ET.parse(f_xml).getroot()[0]
136                 for doc in root.findall('doc'):
137                     for e in doc.findall('seg'):
138                         fd_txt.write(e.text.strip() + '\n')
139         xml_tags = ['<url', '<keywords', '<talkid', '<description',
140                     '<reviewer', '<translator', '<title', '<speaker']
141         for f_orig in glob.iglob(os.path.join(path, 'train.tags*')):
142             print(f_orig)
143             f_txt = f_orig.replace('.tags', '')
144             with io.open(f_txt,mode='w',encoding='utf-8') as fd_txt, io.open(f_orig,mode='r',encoding='utf=8') as fd_orig: <--- INSERT
145             #with open(f_txt, 'w') as fd_txt, open(f_orig) as fd_orig: <--- COMMENT
146                 for l in fd_orig:
147                     if not any(tag in l for tag in xml_tags):
148                         fd_txt.write(l.strip() + '\n')
```
@jekbradbury, you were correct in pull #52 that I didn't need the middle block explicitly encoding/decoding (not seen here) since the file is already open as utf-8.

issue_title: CLI: Defaults of zhmc partition create do not work
issue_body:
### Actual behavior

The command:

    zhmc partition create <cec-name> --name <part-name> --ifl-processors 4

fails with:

    Error: HTTPError: 400,5: Required field(s) 'initial-memory', 'maximum-memory', missing
    from object definition.
    [POST /api/cpcs/fa1f2466-12df-311a-804c-4ed2cc1d6564/partitions]

The zhmc help text suggests that `initial-memory` and `maximum-memory` each have a default of 1024 MB.

Logging the partition creation with `hmc=debug` reveals that the request body of the POST method that creates the partition indeed does not have any of these properties:

    DEBUG zhmcclient.hmc: HMC request: POST https://9.152.151.49:6794/api/cpcs/fa1f2466-12df-311a-804c-4ed2cc1d6564/partitions,
    headers: {
        'X-API-Session': ...,
        'Content-type': 'application/json',
        'Accept': '*/*'
    },
    content(max.1000): '{"ifl-processors": 4, "name": "simon_vnics"}'

    DEBUG zhmcclient.hmc: HMC response: POST https://9.152.151.49:6794/api/cpcs/fa1f2466-12df-311a-804c-4ed2cc1d6564/partitions,
    status: 400,
    headers: {
        'x-request-id': ...,
        'Content-Length': '549',
        'Server': 'zSeries management console API web server / 2.0',
        'Connection': 'close',
        'Cache-control': 'no-cache',
        'Date': 'Wed, 19 Apr 2017 09:10:02 GMT',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
    content(max.1000): '{"http-status":400,"reason":5,"request-uri":"/api/cpcs/fa1f2466-12df-311a-804c-4ed2cc1d6564/partitions","request-method":"POST","message":"Required field(s) \'initial-memory\', \'maximum-memory\', missing from object definition.","request-headers":{"content-type":"application/json","connection":"keep-alive","host":"9.152.151.49:6794","content-length":"44","accept":"*/*","x-api-session":"...","user-agent":"python-requests/2.13.0","accept-encoding":"gzip, deflate"},"request-authenticated-as":"ensadmin"}'

### Expected behavior

The stated defaults for `initial-memory` and `maximum-memory`should be applied when the respective options are not specified.

For that to happen, the CLI layer needs to add them to the list of input properties for the create partition method.

### Execution environment

* zhmcclient version: 0.12.0.dev3
* Operating system (type+version): RHEL 7.2
* HMC version: 2.13.1
* CPC version: 2.13.1

issue_title: [webui] Allow timeline scroll-to-zoom without holding ALT
Actor destructor was not called after the driver was killed
issue_body:
I installed ray from the latest source code with python 3.5.4 on ubuntu 16.04.

I defined a Runner used to be an actor running on workers. In the Runner's constructor, an BasicSC2Env is inited and it is supposed to be destructed when the Runner actor is destructed.
```
class Runner(object):
    def __del__(self):
        print("Runner del")
        self.env.close()
        print("Runner del finished")

    def __init__(self, actor_id, config, visualize=False):
        ...
        self.env = ppo_environment.BasicSC2Env(
            map_name=self.config['map'],
            agent_race=None,
            bot_race=None,
            difficulty=None,
            step_mul=self.step_mul,
            screen_size_px=(self.screen_size, self.screen_size),
            minimap_size_px=(self.screen_size, self.screen_size),
            visualize=self.visualize,
            num_agents=self.num_units,
            win_len=self.win_len,
            num_g_channel=self.config['num_g_channel'],
            num_l_channel=self.config['num_l_channel'])
        #atexit.register(self.env.close)
```
I added some logs to Worker's method main_loop in worker.py.
```
1046     def main_loop(self):
1047         """The main loop a worker runs to receive and execute tasks."""
1048
1049         def exit(signum, frame):
1050             import traceback
1051             traceback.print_stack()
1052             print("exit with signal:{} time:{}".format(signum, time.time()))
1053             cleanup(worker=self)
1054             print("cleanup finished time:{}".format(time.time()))
1055             sys.exit(0)
1056
1057         signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, exit)
1058
1059         check_main_thread()
1060         while True:
1061             print("get_task time: {}".format(time.time()))
1062             task = self._get_next_task_from_local_scheduler()
1063             print("process task start time: {}".format(time.time()))
1064             self._wait_for_and_process_task(task)
1065             print("process task end time: {}".format(time.time()))
```

But I found the following problems:
1. When I killed the driver, Runner::__del__ is never called, so self.env is never destruced.
2. When I added a line atexit.register(self.env.close), self.env.close was called in certain situations. While in other situations, I got the log "get_task time: {}" but no "process task start time: {}", which means the worker was blocked at "task = self._get_next_task_from_local_scheduler()", and self.env.close was not called.

Thanks for helping.

issue_title: Reliance on node identifiers in decanonicalization of complex/composite
issue_body: When decanonicalizing the node representing a complex or composite, the code tries to get information about the latent node tuple, which is bad because this is just a clever way to maintain consistent and unique identifiers. If one chooses to hash these tuples, then this decanonicalization breaks, when it definitely shouldn't.

issue_title: pypi packaging
In GFS, set_model_grid should be the method to return grid variables
issue_body: Currently in the GFS pyx wrapper, `init_model` is what returns e.g. latitude and longitude grids, while `set_model_grid` is what actually generates those grids. Instead, `set_model_grid` should return those grids.

issue_title: [V3] Can't eval a print with codeblocks
issue_body:
# Command bugs

#### Command name

`[p]eval`

#### What cog is this command from?

dev_commands

#### What were you expecting to happen?

I'm expecting the following answer in a message:
```py
test
```

#### What actually happened?

I got the following answer in a message:
```py
async def func():
                 ^
SyntaxError: unexpected EOF while parsing (<string>, line 1)
```

#### How can we reproduce this issue?

1. Run an instance of RedV3 on which you are a dev with `--dev`
2. Execute the following command:
```
[p]eval ```py
print("test")```
```

The problem occurs because the line with the trailing ``` is completely removed from the executed code.  
This can be checked by adding statements before the print. If, for example, you add a second print before the existing one, only the first one will be printed and there will be no error.

This is an issue because forgetting a linefeed produces unexpected results. That last line is visible in the codeblock sent to the bot, yet it isn't executed. It also completely crashes the command if there's only one statement.

issue_title: serializers returning an object with buffer key in it get treated as buffers
issue_body:
https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/24f3312897f2694f10fddd207206af0aae10ecce/packages/base/src/utils.ts#L184 and https://github.com/jupyter-widgets/ipywidgets/blob/24f3312897f2694f10fddd207206af0aae10ecce/packages/base/src/utils.ts#L155 are too generous, they should whitelist specific types etc, I noticed this when I returned 
`{'buffer': ar.data.buffer, dtype:arrayToTypes[ar.data.constructor.name], shape:ar.shape}` from a serializer. On the python side I got passed a memory object, instead of a dict.
Might be worth to take a look at https://github.com/jashkenas/underscore/pull/2694/files#diff-0f36b362a0b81d6f4d4bfd8a7413c75dR62 for solving this.

issue_title: observe() in a loop doesn't work properly
issue_body:
The following code has 4 widgets. The first two are generated by a loop and don't work properly. When you click the first widget, it claims you clicked the second widget . The last two widgets work fine

```
import ipywidgets as widgets
from IPython.display import Image, display, clear_output

# a and b in loop
def wi_do(title, change):
    print(title, change, "\n")
for title in ['a', 'b']:
    wi = widgets.SelectMultiple(
                options= ['one', 'two'],
                value= ['one'],
                description=title
            )
    display(wi)
    wi.observe(lambda x: wi_do(title, x), names='value')
    
# A
A = widgets.SelectMultiple(
            options= ['one', 'two'],
            value= ['one'],
            description='A'
        )
display(A)
def A_do(change):
    print(A.description, change, "\n")
A.observe(A_do, names='value')

# B
B = widgets.SelectMultiple(
            options= ['one', 'two'],
            value= ['one'],
            description='B'
        )
display(B)
def B_do(change):
    print(B.description, change, "\n")
B.observe(B_do, names='value')
```

issue_title: Warn user when applying a duplication

issue_title: Flag usage
issue_body:
I've been experimenting with the flags module and had a question. From the documentation:

> """This package is used to define and parse command line flags.
This package defines a *distributed* flag-definition policy: rather than
an application having to define all flags in or near main(), each Python
module defines flags that are useful to it.  When one Python module
imports another, it gains access to the other's flags.  (This is
implemented by having all modules share a common, global registry object
containing all the flag information.)
Flags are defined through the use of one of the DEFINE_xxx functions.
The specific function used determines how the flag is parsed, checked,
and optionally type-converted, when it's seen on the command line.
"""

If flags are defined in different packages `__init__.py` files, how are they discovered prior to parsing the flags? For instance, assume a standard project:

`module/__init__.py`

```python
if __name__ == "__main__":
  FLAGS(argv)
  server.start()
```

And our server object uses a library called foo that defines a flag that is not imported until the server is actually invoked:

`module/foo/__init__.py`
```python
DEFINE_string('debug', None, 'Debug mode')
```

This will raise an error as the flag definition will not be known by flags yet. I'm probably missing something here - looking for guidance.

issue_title: Improve documentation and flow
Tokenizer.fit_on_text splits 1 string into chars when char_level=False
issue_body:
From: https://github.com/keras-team/keras/issues/10768 by @hadaev8


Tokenizer will fit/transform the string into chars if a string is provided to `fit_on_texts`/`text_to_sequences` methods regardless of char_level setting. This is happening because the method expects a list of strings and is splitting the string into chars if just 1 string is given in this line for fitting:
https://github.com/keras-team/keras-preprocessing/blob/e002ebd40e888965686e8946acefe02f5a910576/keras_preprocessing/text.py#L205

and this one for trasnforming: 
https://github.com/keras-team/keras-preprocessing/blob/e002ebd40e888965686e8946acefe02f5a910576/keras_preprocessing/text.py#L293

Reproducible code illustrating the problem with fit_on_texts:
```
from keras.preprocessing.text import Tokenizer
text='check check fail'
tokenizer = Tokenizer()
tokenizer.fit_on_texts(text)
tokenizer.word_index
```
Output: 
```
{'c': 1, 'h': 2, 'e': 3, 'k': 4, 'f': 5, 'a': 6, 'i': 7, 'l': 8}
```
wrapping text into list solves the issue 
```
tokenizer.fit_on_texts([text])
tokenizer.word_index
```
`{'check': 1, 'fail': 2}`

I can recommend checking that text is a list of strings and if it is not producing a warning and wrapping it into the list or erroring out

issue_title: Can't build against RHEL cfitsio
issue_body:
Redhat Enterprise Linux (and derived distributions like Fedora) install libcfitsio.a in /usr/lib/ and /usr/lib64, but place fitsio.h in /usr/include/cfitsio/. The current build system expects a common root directory for include/fitsio.h and lib/libcfitsio.a.

Should scons be more flexible about this or should we just ignore this since we want to get rid of cfitsio anyway?

issue_title: Literal(True) creates literals of type xsd:integer not xsd:boolean
issue_body:
cuu...@gmail.com, 2009-10-29T12:57:32.000Z 
What steps will reproduce the problem?
1. print Literal(True).datatype

What is the expected output? What do you see instead?

Datatype should be xsd:boolean but it is xsd:integer. This bug is related
to the fact that in python "bool" is subclass of "int", and Literal first
does "isinstance(obj, int)" and only then "isinstance(obj, bool)". Patch
attached.

What version of the product are you using? On what operating system?

rdflib 2.4.2. This issue seems to be already fixed in 2.5.x
